content.content:
{"body":"This PR includes some work from [this RFC](https://community.tuist.dev/t/rfc-comparison-primitives-for-mcp-cli-and-agent-skills/925). I'm adding tools and prompts for agents to get and work with the data structures of Tuist.

You can use the deployed preview to play with this locally:

```bash
claude mcp add --transport http tuist https://tuist-preview-3jwd-pr-9732.onrender.com/mcp
```

> [!NOTE]
> Once we publish this, I'll write a blog post. The idea for us is to continue to iterate on the prompts and tools based on the questions we see our users wanting to answer.

> [!NOTE]
> I left Gradle-related tools and prompts out of the scope to keep the PR size manageable. I also left skills, CLI, and API out of the scope for similar reasons.

## Thoughts on skills, CLI, & mcp
Having to support either skill + CLI command and MCP (tools & prompts) feels wrong, and both approaches have upsides and downsides. The upside is that coding agents make the duplications more manageable, so I suggest that we stick with this model. It also forces us to expose all our data through the API and CLI, so people can also use it for other things not just in the context of a     69→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareBuilds do
    70→+  @moduledoc """
    71→+  Guides you through comparing two build runs to identify performance regressions, cache changes, and build issues. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if base or head is a dashboard URL.
    72→+  """
    73→+
    74→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt
    75→+
    76→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
    77→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport
    78→+
    79→+  schema do
    80→+    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    81→+
    82→+    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."
    83→+
    84→+    field :base, :string,
    85→+      description: """
    86→+      Base build: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. \
    87→+      Defaults to the latest build on the project's default branch when omitted.\
    88→+      """
    89→+
    90→+    field :head, :string,
    91→+      description: """
    92→+      Head build: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. \
    93→+      This is the build you want to evaluate. When provided without a base, \
    94→+      the base defaults to the latest build on the project's default branch.\
    95→+      """
    96→+  end
    97→+
    98→+  @impl true
    99→+  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
   100→+    base = Map.get(arguments, :base)
   101→+    head = Map.get(arguments, :head)
   102→+    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
   103→+    meta = PromptSupport.resolve_project_metadata(account_handle, project_handle)
   104→+
   105→+    response =
   106→+      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
   107→+        "type" => "text",
   108→+        "text" => prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, meta)
   109→+      })
   110→+
   111→+    {:reply, response, frame}
   112→+  end
   113→+
   114→+  defp prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, meta) do
   115→+    default_branch = meta.default_branch
   116→+    build_system = meta.build_system
   117→+    resolution = resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch, build_system)
   118→+    tools_section = tools_section(build_system)
   119→+    workflow_section = workflow_section(build_system)
   120→+
   121→+    """
   122→+    # Compare Builds
   123→+
   124→+    Use MCP tools to fetch two build runs and compare them to identify regressions and improvements.
   125→+
   126→+    #{tools_section}
   127→+
   128→+    ## Workflow
   129→+
   130→+    #{resolution}
   131→+
   132→+    #{workflow_section}
   133→+
   134→+    ### Summary format
   135→+
   136→+    Produce a structured summary with:
   137→+    1. **Overall**: duration change, status change, pass/regress verdict.
   138→+    2. **Top regressions**: up to 5 items sorted by absolute time increase.
   139→+    3. **Cache changes**: hit-rate delta, notable changes.
   140→+    4. **New issues**: new errors and warnings (Xcode only).
   141→+    5. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes.
   142→+    """
   143→+  end
   144→+
   145→+  defp tools_section(:gradle) do
   146→+    """
   147→+    ## Available MCP tools
   148→+
   149→+    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects (includes build_system field).
   150→+    - **list_gradle_builds**: List Gradle build runs for a project (supports git_branch filter).
   151→+    - **get_gradle_build**: Get detailed metrics for a Gradle build run.
   152→+    - **list_gradle_build_tasks**: List Gradle tasks with outcomes and cache status.
   153→+    """
   154→+  end
   155→+
   156→+  defp tools_section(_xcode_or_unknown) do
   157→+    """
   158→+    ## Available MCP tools
   159→+
   160→+    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects (includes build_system field).
   161→+    - **list_xcode_builds**: List Xcode build runs for a project (supports git_branch, status, scheme filters).
   162→+    - **get_xcode_build**: Get detailed metrics for an Xcode build run (by ID or dashboard URL).
   163→+    - **list_xcode_build_targets**: List per-target build and compilation durations.
   164→+    - **list_xcode_build_files**: List per-file compilation durations (sorted slowest-first by default).
   165→+    - **list_xcode_build_issues**: List warnings and errors.
   166→+    - **list_xcode_build_cache_tasks**: List cache hit/miss status per task.
   167→+    - **list_xcode_build_cas_outputs**: List CAS upload/download operations.
   168→+    """
   169→+  end
   170→+
   171→+  defp workflow_section(:gradle) do
   172→+    """
   173→+    ### 2. Compare top-level metrics
   174→+
   175→+    Compare these fields between the two Gradle builds:
   176→+    - **duration**: Total build time. Flag regressions over 10%.
   177→+    - **status**: Did either build fail?
   178→+    - **tasks_local_hit_count**, **tasks_remote_hit_count**, **tasks_executed_count**: Cache effectiveness changes.
   179→+
   180→+    ### 3. Drill into tasks (if duration regressed)
   181→+
   182→+    Use `list_gradle_build_tasks` for both builds. Compare `duration_ms` and `outcome` per task.
   183→+    Identify tasks that changed from `local_hit`/`remote_hit` to `executed` (cache misses).
   184→+    Sort by absolute time difference.
   185→+
   186→+    ### 4. Check cache changes
   187→+
   188→+    Compare task-level cache behavior:
   189→+    - Tasks that changed from hit to executed (potential cache invalidation).
   190→+    - Tasks that changed from executed to hit (improvements).
   191→+    - New tasks that appeared in the head build.
   192→+    """
   193→+  end
   194→+
   195→+  defp workflow_section(_xcode_or_unknown) do
   196→+    """
   197→+    ### 2. Compare top-level metrics
   198→+
   199→+    Compare these fields between the two Xcode builds:
   200→+    - **duration**: Total build time. Flag regressions over 10%.
   201→+    - **status**: Did either build fail?
   202→+    - **cacheable_tasks_count**, **cacheable_task_local_hits_count**, **cacheable_task_remote_hits_count**: Cache effectiveness changes.
   203→+    - **category**: clean vs. incremental (only compare like-for-like).
   204→+
   205→+    ### 3. Drill into targets (if duration regressed)
   206→+
   207→+    Use `list_xcode_build_targets` for both builds. Compare `build_duration` and `compilation_duration` per target.
   208→+    Identify which targets regressed the most and sort by absolute time difference.
   209→+
   210→+    ### 4. Drill into files (if a target regressed)
   211→+
   212→+    Use `list_xcode_build_files` filtered by the regressed target. Compare `compilation_duration` per file.
   213→+    Identify the slowest files and any new files that appeared in the head build.
   214→+
   215→+    ### 5. Check issues
   216→+
   217→+    Use `list_xcode_build_issues` for both builds. Report:
   218→+    - New errors in head that were not in base.
   219→+    - New warnings introduced.
   220→+    - Issues that were resolved.
   221→+
   222→+    ### 6. Check cache changes
   223→+
   224→+    Use `list_xcode_build_cache_tasks` for both builds. Report:
   225→+    - Tasks that changed from hit to miss (potential cache invalidation).
   226→+    - Tasks that changed from miss to hit (improvements).
   227→+    """
   228→+  end
   229→+
   230→+  defp resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch, build_system) do
   231→+    branch = default_branch || "main"
   232→+    {list_tool, get_tool} = build_tool_names(build_system)
   233→+
   234→+    project_line =
   235→+      if account_handle && project_handle,
   236→+        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
   237→+        else: ""
   238→+
   239→+    base_instruction =
   240→+      case base do
   241→+        nil ->
   242→+          "For the **base**, use `#{list_tool}` with `git_branch=#{branch}` and pick the most recent build."
   243→+
   244→+        _ ->
   245→+          "For the **base**, use `#{get_tool}` with `build_run_id=#{base}`."
   246→+      end
   247→+
   248→+    head_instruction =
   249→+      case head do
   250→+        nil ->
   251→+          "For the **head**, detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). " <>
   252→+            "If it differs from `#{branch}`, use `#{list_tool}` with that branch and pick the most recent build. " <>
   253→+            "If it matches the base branch, ask the user which build to compare."
   254→+
   255→+        _ ->
   256→+          "For the **head**, use `#{get_tool}` with `build_run_id=#{head}`."
   257→+      end
   258→+
   259→+    """
   260→+    ### 1. Resolve builds
   261→+
   262→+    #{project_line}#{base_instruction}
   263→+    #{head_instruction}
   264→+    """
   265→+  end
   266→+
   267→+  defp build_tool_names(:gradle), do: {"list_gradle_builds", "get_gradle_build"}
   268→+  defp build_tool_names(_), do: {"list_xcode_builds", "get_xcode_build"}
   269→+end

   276→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareBundles do
   277→+  @moduledoc """
   278→+  Guides you through comparing two bundles to identify size changes across the artifact tree. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if base or head is a dashboard URL.
   279→+  """
   280→+
   281→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt
   282→+
   283→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
   284→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport
   285→+
   286→+  schema do
   287→+    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
   288→+
   289→+    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."
   290→+
   291→+    field :base, :string,
   292→+      description: """
   293→+      Base bundle: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. \
   294→+      Defaults to the latest bundle on the project's default branch when omitted.\
   295→+      """
   296→+
   297→+    field :head, :string,
   298→+      description: """
   299→+      Head bundle: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. \
   300→+      This is the bundle you want to evaluate. When provided without a base, \
   301→+      the base defaults to the latest bundle on the project's default branch.\
   302→+      """
   303→+  end
   304→+
   305→+  @impl true
   306→+  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
   307→+    base = Map.get(arguments, :base)
   308→+    head = Map.get(arguments, :head)
   309→+    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
   310→+    default_branch = PromptSupport.resolve_default_branch(account_handle, project_handle)
   311→+
   312→+    response =
   313→+      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
   314→+        "type" => "text",
   315→+        "text" => prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
   316→+      })
   317→+
   318→+    {:reply, response, frame}
   319→+  end
   320→+
   321→+  defp prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
   322→+    resolution = resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
   323→+
   324→+    """
   325→+    # Compare Bundles
   326→+
   327→+    Use MCP tools to fetch two bundles and compare their size and artifact composition.
   328→+
   329→+    ## Available MCP tools
   330→+
   331→+    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects.
   332→+    - **list_bundles**: List bundles for a project (supports git_branch filter).
   333→+    - **get_bundle**: Get bundle metadata (sizes, version, platforms).
   334→+    - **get_bundle_artifact_tree**: Get the full artifact tree for a bundle as a flat list sorted by path. Each artifact includes `artifact_type`, `path`, and `size`.
   335→+
   336→+    ## Workflow
   337→+
   338→+    #{resolution}
   339→+
   340→+    ### 2. Compare top-level sizes
   341→+
   342→+    Compare these fields between the two bundles:
   343→+    - **install_size**: The installed size on device. Flag increases over 5%.
   344→+    - **download_size**: The download size from the store. Flag increases over 5%.
   345→+    - **version**: Note version changes.
   346→+    - **supported_platforms**: Note platform changes.
   347→+
   348→+    ### 3. Compare artifact trees
   349→+
   350→+    Use `get_bundle_artifact_tree` for both the base and head bundles. Save each result to a local JSON file so you can operate on the data without further API calls.
   351→+
   352→+    Once you have both artifact trees locally:
   353→+    - Match artifacts by `path` between base and head.
   354→+    - Calculate size delta for each matched artifact.
   355→+    - Identify new artifacts in head that don't exist in base.
   356→+    - Identify removed artifacts that were in base but not in head.
   357→+    - Sort artifacts by absolute size increase to find the biggest contributors.
   358→+
   359→+    ### Summary format
   360→+
   361→+    Produce a structured summary with:
   362→+    1. **Overall**: install_size delta, download_size delta, verdict (acceptable/concerning).
   363→+    2. **Top size increases**: up to 10 artifacts with the largest size growth.
   364→+    3. **New artifacts**: artifacts added in the head bundle.
   365→+    4. **Removed artifacts**: artifacts removed from the base bundle.
   366→+    5. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes (e.g., "remove unused assets in Resources/", "optimize image at path/to/large.png").
   367→+    """
   368→+  end
   369→+
   370→+  defp resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
   371→+    branch = default_branch || "main"
   372→+
   373→+    project_line =
   374→+      if account_handle && project_handle,
   375→+        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
   376→+        else: ""
   377→+
   378→+    base_instruction =
   379→+      case base do
   380→+        nil ->
   381→+          "For the **base**, use `list_bundles` with `git_branch=#{branch}` and pick the most recent bundle."
   382→+
   383→+        _ ->
   384→+          "For the **base**, use `get_bundle` with `bundle_id=#{base}`."
   385→+      end
   386→+
   387→+    head_instruction =
   388→+      case head do
   389→+        nil ->
   390→+          "For the **head**, detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). " <>
   391→+            "If it differs from `#{branch}`, use `list_bundles` with that branch and pick the most recent bundle. " <>
   392→+            "If it matches the base branch, ask the user which bundle to compare."
   393→+
   394→+        _ ->
   395→+          "For the **head**, use `get_bundle` with `bundle_id=#{head}`."
   396→+      end
   397→+
   398→+    """
   399→+    ### 1. Resolve bundles
   400→+
   401→+    #{project_line}#{base_instruction}
   402→+    #{head_instruction}
   403→+    """
   404→+  end
   405→+end

   412→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareCacheRuns do
   413→+  @moduledoc """
   414→+  Guides you through comparing two cache runs to identify cache effectiveness changes and target-level regressions. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if base or head is a dashboard URL.
   415→+  """
   416→+
   417→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt
   418→+
   419→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
   420→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport
   421→+
   422→+  schema do
   423→+    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
   424→+
   425→+    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."
   426→+
   427→+    field :base, :string,
   428→+      description:

   432→+    field :head, :string,
   433→+      description:
   434→+        "Head cache run: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. " <>
   435→+          "This is the cache run you want to evaluate. When provided without a base, " <>
   436→+          "the base defaults to the latest cache run on the project's default branch."
   437→+  end
   438→+
   439→+  @impl true
   440→+  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
   441→+    base = Map.get(arguments, :base)
   442→+    head = Map.get(arguments, :head)
   443→+    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
   444→+    default_branch = PromptSupport.resolve_default_branch(account_handle, project_handle)
   445→+
   446→+    response =
   447→+      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
   448→+        "type" => "text",
   449→+        "text" => prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
   450→+      })
   451→+
   452→+    {:reply, response, frame}
   453→+  end
   454→+
   455→+  defp prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
   456→+    resolution = resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
   457→+
   458→+    """
   459→+    # Compare Cache Runs
   460→+
   461→+    Use MCP tools to fetch two cache runs and compare their effectiveness and target-level cache behavior.
   462→+
   463→+    ## Available MCP tools
   464→+
   465→+    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects.
   466→+    - **list_cache_runs**: List cache runs for a project (supports git_branch filter).
   467→+    - **get_cache_run**: Get detailed cache run info (by ID or dashboard URL).
   468→+    - **list_xcode_module_cache_targets**: List per-target module cache hit/miss status and subhashes.
   469→+
   470→+    ## Workflow
   471→+
   472→+    #{resolution}
   473→+
   474→+    ### 2. Compare top-level metrics
   475→+
   476→+    Compare these fields between the two cache runs:
   477→+    - **duration**: Total cache run time. Flag regressions over 10%.
   478→+    - **status**: Did either cache run fail?
   479→+    - **cacheable_targets**: Total number of cacheable targets.
   480→+    - **local_cache_target_hits** / **remote_cache_target_hits**: Cache hit counts.
   481→+    - Compute and compare overall cache hit rates.
   482→+
   483→+    ### 3. Drill into module cache targets
   484→+
   485→+    Use `list_xcode_module_cache_targets` for both cache runs. For each target:
   486→+    - Match targets by `name` between base and head.
   487→+    - Compare `cache_status` (miss, local, remote).
   488→+    - Identify targets that changed from hit to miss (cache invalidation).
   489→+    - Identify targets that changed from miss to hit (improvements).
   490→+    - Compare `subhashes` to identify which component changed and caused a cache miss.
   491→+
   492→+    ### 4. Analyze cache invalidation and find the root cause
   493→+
   494→+    For targets that went from hit to miss, compare their `subhashes` (`sources`, `resources`, `dependencies`, `target_settings`, `project_settings`, `deployment_target`, `info_plist`, `entitlements`, `headers`, `copy_files`, `core_data_models`, `target_scripts`, `buildable_folders`, `external`).
   495→+
   496→+    **Identify the root cause target.** When many targets are invalidated, it is usually because one target changed and its dependents were invalidated through the `dependencies` subhash cascading down. To find the root cause:
   497→+    - Look for the target(s) where a subhash **other than** `dependencies` changed (e.g., `sources`, `target_settings`, `resources`). That is the target where the actual change happened.
   498→+    - Targets where **only** the `dependencies` subhash changed were invalidated because they depend (directly or transitively) on the root cause target.
   499→+    - Use target names to infer the dependency topology. For example, a target named `FeatureLogin` likely depends on `Core` or `Networking`. Leaf/feature targets depend on shared/core targets. Work from the bottom of the graph upward.
   500→+    - If multiple non-dependency subhashes changed across different targets, there may be multiple independent root causes.

500→+    - If multiple non-dependency subhashes changed across different targets, there may be multiple independent root causes.
   501→+
   502→+    ### Summary format
   503→+
   504→+    Produce a structured summary with:
   505→+    1. **Overall**: duration change, cache hit rate delta, verdict (improved/regressed/stable).
   506→+    2. **Root cause**: the target(s) and specific subhash(es) that triggered the cascade. Explain what likely changed (e.g., "sources changed in Core, which invalidated 12 dependent targets through the dependencies hash").
   507→+    3. **Cache regressions**: targets that went from hit to miss, grouped by root cause.
   508→+    4. **Cache improvements**: targets that went from miss to hit.
   509→+    5. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes (e.g., "the source change in Core invalidated 12 targets -- consider whether that change is necessary, or if it can be scoped to avoid touching Core's public interface").
   510→+    """
   511→+  end
   512→+
   513→+  defp resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
   514→+    branch = default_branch || "main"
   515→+
   516→+    project_line =
   517→+      if account_handle && project_handle,
   518→+        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
   519→+        else: ""
   520→+
   521→+    base_instruction =
   522→+      case base do
   523→+        nil ->
   524→+          "For the **base**, use `list_cache_runs` with `git_branch=#{branch}` and pick the most recent cache run."
   525→+
   526→+        _ ->
   527→+          "For the **base**, use `get_cache_run` with `cache_run_id=#{base}`."
   528→+      end
   529→+
   530→+    head_instruction =
   531→+      case head do
   532→+        nil ->
   533→+          "For the **head**, detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). " <>
   534→+            "If it differs from `#{branch}`, use `list_cache_runs` with that branch and pick the most recent cache run. " <>
   535→+            "If it matches the base branch, ask the user which cache run to compare."
   536→+
   537→+        _ ->
   538→+          "For the **head**, use `get_cache_run` with `cache_run_id=#{head}`."
   539→+      end
   540→+
   541→+    """
   542→+    ### 1. Resolve cache runs
   543→+
   544→+    #{project_line}#{base_instruction}
   545→+    #{head_instruction}
   546→+    """
   547→+  end
   548→+end

   698→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareTestCase do
   699→+  @moduledoc """
   700→+  Guides you through comparing a test case's behavior across two branches or time periods. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if test_case_id is a dashboard URL.
   701→+  """
   702→+
   703→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt
   704→+
   705→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
   706→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport
   707→+
   708→+  schema do
   709→+    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
   710→+
   711→+    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."
   712→+
   713→+    field :test_case_id, :string, description: "The test case ID or a Tuist dashboard URL."
   714→+
   715→+    field :base_branch, :string,
   716→+      description: "The base branch to compare against (defaults to the project's default branch)."
   717→+
   718→+    field :head_branch, :string, description: "The head branch to evaluate."
   719→+  end
   720→+
   721→+  @impl true
   722→+  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
   723→+    test_case_id = Map.get(arguments, :test_case_id)
   724→+    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
   725→+    default_branch = PromptSupport.resolve_default_branch(account_handle, project_handle)
   726→+    base_branch = Map.get(arguments, :base_branch) || default_branch || "main"
   727→+    head_branch = Map.get(arguments, :head_branch)
   728→+
   729→+    response =
   730→+      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
   731→+        "type" => "text",
   732→+        "text" => prompt_text(test_case_id, base_branch, head_branch, account_handle, project_handle)
   733→+      })
   734→+
   735→+    {:reply, response, frame}
   736→+  end
   737→+
   738→+  defp prompt_text(test_case_id, base_branch, head_branch, account_handle, project_handle) do
   739→+    resolution = resolution_section(test_case_id, account_handle, project_handle)
   740→+
   741→+    """
   742→+    # Compare Test Case
   743→+
   744→+    Use MCP tools to analyze a test case's behavior across branches and identify regressions or improvements.
   745→+
   746→+    ## Available MCP tools
   747→+
   748→+    - **get_test_case**: Get test case metrics (reliability, flakiness, avg duration).
   749→+    - **list_test_case_runs**: List runs for a test case (supports filtering by test_run_id).
   750→+    - **get_test_case_run**: Get failure details, repetitions for a specific run.
   751→+    - **list_test_case_run_attachments**: List attachments (screenshots, logs, crash reports) for a test case run. Each attachment includes a temporary download URL.
   752→+    - **list_test_runs**: List test runs (supports git_branch filter).
   753→+
   754→+    ## Workflow
   755→+
   756→+    #{resolution}
   757→+
   758→+    ### 2. Get test case metrics
   759→+
   760→+    Use `get_test_case` to understand the overall health:
   761→+    - **reliability_rate**: success rate percentage.
   762→+    - **flakiness_rate**: flaky run percentage in last 30 days.
   763→+    - **total_runs** / **failed_runs**: volume context.
   764→+
   765→+    ### 3. Compare across branches
   766→+
   767→+    Base branch: `#{base_branch}`
   768→+    #{if head_branch, do: "Head branch: `#{head_branch}`", else: "Head branch: detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). If it matches the base branch, ask the user which branch to compare."}
   769→+
   770→+    For each branch:
   771→+    1. Use `list_test_runs` with the branch name to find recent test runs.
   772→+    2. Use `list_test_case_runs` filtered by `test_run_id` to find runs of this test case.
   773→+    3. Compare:
   774→+       - **Pass/fail status**: Is it failing on head but passing on base?
   775→+       - **Duration**: Is it slower on head?
   776→+       - **Flakiness**: Is it flaky on head but stable on base?
   777→+
   778→+    ### 4. Inspect failures
   779→+
   780→+    If the test case fails on the head branch:
   781→+    1. Use `get_test_case_run` with a failing run ID.
   782→+    2. Examine `failures[].message`, `failures[].path`, `failures[].line_number`.
   783→+    3. Check `repetitions` to see if it's intermittent.
   784→+
   785→+    ### 5. Inspect attachments
   786→+
   787→+    For failing runs, use `list_test_case_run_attachments` to find diagnostic artifacts. Each attachment includes a download URL.
   788→+
   789→+    - **Text attachments** (logs, crash reports, JSON, XML, CSV): download and include relevant excerpts inline.
   790→+    - **Image attachments** (screenshots, PNGs, JPGs): present the download URL so the developer can view them.
   791→+
   792→+    ### 6. Identify root cause
   793→+
   794→+    Based on the comparison:
   795→+    - If newly failing: look at commits between the two branches.
   796→+    - If newly flaky: look for timing, async, or shared-state issues.
   797→+    - If duration regressed: look for added test setup or slow assertions.
   798→+
   799→+    ### Summary format
   800→+
   801→+    Produce a structured summary with:
   802→+    1. **Test case**: name, module, suite.
   803→+    2. **Base branch behavior**: pass rate, avg duration, flakiness.
   804→+    3. **Head branch behavior**: pass rate, avg duration, flakiness.
   805→+    4. **Verdict**: regressed / improved / stable.
   806→+    5. **Root cause hypothesis**: what likely changed.
   807→+    6. **Recommendations**: fix steps or further investigation needed. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes with file paths and line numbers when available.
   808→+    """
   809→+  end
   810→+
   811→+  defp resolution_section(nil, _account_handle, _project_handle) do
   812→+    """
   813→+    ### 1. Resolve the test case
   814→+
   815→+    Ask the user for the test case to compare, or use `list_test_cases` with `flaky=true` to find candidates.
   816→+    """
   817→+  end
   818→+
   819→+  defp resolution_section(test_case_id, _account_handle, _project_handle) when is_binary(test_case_id) do
   820→+    """
   821→+    ### 1. Resolve the test case
   822→+
   823→+    Use `get_test_case` with `test_case_id=#{test_case_id}` to fetch the test case details.
   824→+    """
   825→+  end
   826→+
   827→+  defp resolution_section(_, _, _) do
   828→+    resolution_section(nil, nil, nil)
   829→+  end
   830→+end

   882→+
   883→+    """
   884→+    # Compare Test Runs
   885→+
   886→+    Use MCP tools to fetch two test runs and compare them to identify new failures, flaky tests, and performance changes.
   887→+
   888→+    ## Available MCP tools
   889→+
   890→+    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects.
   891→+    - **list_test_runs**: List test runs for a project (supports git_branch, status, scheme filters).
   892→+    - **get_test_run**: Get detailed metrics for a test run (total/failed/flaky counts, duration).
   893→+    - **list_test_module_runs**: List per-module results within a test run.
   894→+    - **list_test_suite_runs**: List per-suite results (optionally filtered by module).
   895→+    - **list_test_case_runs**: List individual test case runs (supports test_run_id filter).
   896→+    - **get_test_case_run**: Get failure details and repetitions for a specific test case run.
   897→+    - **list_test_case_run_attachments**: List attachments (screenshots, logs, crash reports) for a test case run. Each attachment includes a temporary download URL.
   898→+
   899→+    ## Workflow
   900→+
   901→+    #{resolution}
   902→+
   903→+    ### 2. Compare top-level metrics
   904→+
   905→+    Compare these fields between the two test runs:
   906→+    - **duration**: Total test duration. Flag regressions over 10%.
   907→+    - **total_test_count**: Did the test count change?
   908→+    - **failed_test_count**: New failures?
   909→+    - **flaky_test_count**: New flaky tests?
   910→+    - **status**: Did either run fail entirely?
   911→+
   912→+    ### 3. Drill into modules (if failures increased)
   913→+
   914→+    Use `list_test_module_runs` for both runs. Compare `status`, `duration`, and `test_case_count` per module.
   915→+    Identify modules that went from passing to failing, or that significantly increased in duration.
   916→+
   917→+    ### 4. Drill into suites (for regressed modules)
   918→+
   919→+    Use `list_test_suite_runs` filtered by the regressed module. Compare individual suite results.
   920→+
   921→+    ### 5. Inspect failing test cases
   922→+
   923→+    Use `list_test_case_runs` with `test_run_id` for the head run to find failures.
   924→+    Use `get_test_case_run` to get failure messages, file paths, and line numbers.
   925→+
   926→+    Compare with the base run to identify:
   927→+    - **New failures**: tests that passed in base but failed in head.
   928→+    - **Fixed tests**: tests that failed in base but passed in head.
   929→+    - **Persistent failures**: tests that failed in both runs.
   930→+
   931→+    ### 6. Check for flaky tests
   932→+
   933→+    Look at tests with `is_flaky: true` in the head run. Use `get_test_case_run` to inspect repetitions.
   934→+    Report any newly flaky tests that were stable in the base run.
   935→+
   936→+    ### 7. Inspect attachments
   937→+
   938→+    For failing or flaky test case runs, use `list_test_case_run_attachments` to find diagnostic artifacts. Each attachment includes a download URL.
   939→+
   940→+    - **Text attachments** (logs, crash reports, JSON, XML, CSV): download and include relevant excerpts inline.
   941→+    - **Image attachments** (screenshots, PNGs, JPGs): present the download URL so the developer can view them. Describe what the image likely shows based on its file name and context.
   942→+
   943→+    ### Summary format
   944→+
   945→+    Produce a structured summary with:
   946→+    1. **Overall**: duration change, test count change, pass/fail verdict.
   947→+    2. **New failures**: list of tests that newly failed, with failure messages.
   948→+    3. **New flaky tests**: tests that became flaky.
   949→+    4. **Fixed tests**: tests that were previously failing and now pass.
   950→+    5. **Duration regressions**: modules or suites with notable slowdowns.
   951→+    6. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes (e.g., "investigate ModuleX failures at path:line", "fix flaky test Y by addressing shared state").
   952→+    """
   953→+  end
   954→+
   955→+  defp resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
   956→+    branch = default_branch || "main"
   957→+
   958→+    project_line =
   959→+      if account_handle && project_handle,
   960→+        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
   961→+        else: ""
   962→+
   963→+    base_instruction =
   964→+      case base do
   965→+        nil ->
   966→+          "For the **base**, use `list_test_runs` with `git_branch=#{branch}` and pick the most recent run."
   967→+
   968→+        _ ->
   969→+          "For the **base**, use `get_test_run` with `test_run_id=#{base}`."
   970→+      end
   971→+
   972→+    head_instruction =
   973→+      case head do
   974→+        nil ->
   975→+          "For the **head**, detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). " <>
   976→+            "If it differs from `#{branch}`, use `list_test_runs` with that branch and pick the most recent run. " <>
   977→+            "If it matches the base branch, ask the user which test run to compare."
   978→+
   979→+        _ ->
   980→+          "For the **head**, use `get_test_run` with `test_run_id=#{head}`."
   981→+      end
   982→+
   983→+    """
   984→+    ### 1. Resolve test runs
   985→+
   986→+    #{project_line}#{base_instruction}
   987→+    #{head_instruction}
   988→+    """
   989→+  end
   990→+end

  1228→+  @impl true

  1256→+
  1257→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}

  1267→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetCacheRun do
  1268→+  @moduledoc """
  1269→+  Get detailed information about a specific cache run. The cache_run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/runs/{id}.
  1270→+  """
  1271→+
  1272→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  1273→+
  1274→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  1275→+  alias Tuist.CommandEvents
  1276→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  1277→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
  1278→+
  1279→+  @authorization_action :read
  1280→+  @authorization_category :run
  1281→+
  1282→+  schema do
  1283→+    field :cache_run_id, :string,
  1284→+      required: true,
  1285→+      description: "The ID of the cache run."
  1286→+  end
  1287→+
  1288→+  @impl true
  1289→+  def execute(%{cache_run_id: cache_run_id}, frame) do
  1290→+    with {:ok, event} <-
  1291→+           ToolSupport.load_resource(
  1292→+             get_cache_run(cache_run_id),
  1293→+             "Cache run not found: #{cache_run_id}",
  1294→+             frame
  1295→+           ),
  1296→+         {:ok, _project} <-
  1297→+           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
  1298→+             frame,
  1299→+             event.project_id,
  1300→+             @authorization_action,
  1301→+             @authorization_category
  1302→+           ) do
  1303→+      data = %{
  1304→+        id: event.id,
  1305→+        duration: event.duration,
  1306→+        status: status_to_string(event.status),
  1307→+        tuist_version: event.tuist_version,
  1308→+        swift_version: event.swift_version,
  1309→+        macos_version: event.macos_version,
  1310→+        is_ci: event.is_ci,
  1311→+        git_branch: event.git_branch,
  1312→+        git_commit_sha: event.git_commit_sha,
  1313→+        git_ref: event.git_ref,
  1314→+        command_arguments: event.command_arguments,
  1315→+        cacheable_targets: event.cacheable_targets,
  1316→+        local_cache_target_hits: event.local_cache_target_hits,
  1317→+        remote_cache_target_hits: event.remote_cache_target_hits,
  1318→+        ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(event.created_at, naive: :utc)
  1319→+      }
  1320→+
  1321→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  1322→+    end
  1323→+  end
  1324→+
  1325→+  defp get_cache_run(id) do
  1326→+    case CommandEvents.get_command_event_by_id(id) do
  1327→+      {:ok, event} when event.name == "cache" -> {:ok, event}
  1328→+      {:ok, _event} -> {:error, :not_found}
  1329→+      error -> error
  1330→+    end
  1331→+  end
  1332→+
  1333→+  defp status_to_string(0), do: "success"
  1334→+  defp status_to_string(1), do: "failure"
  1335→+  defp status_to_string(nil), do: "success"
  1336→+  defp status_to_string(status) when is_atom(status), do: Atom.to_string(status)
  1337→+  defp status_to_string(status), do: to_string(status)
  1338→+end

  1547→+  @impl true

  1562→+      data = %{

  1564→+        duration: build.duration,
  1565→+        status: to_string(build.status),
  1566→+        category: if(build.category != "", do: build.category),
  1567→+        scheme: build.scheme,
  1568→+        configuration: build.configuration,
  1569→+        xcode_version: build.xcode_version,

  1599→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListBundles do
  1600→+  @moduledoc """
  1601→+  List bundles (app binaries) for a project. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}.
  1602→+  """
  1603→+
  1604→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  1605→+
  1606→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  1607→+  alias Tuist.Bundles
  1608→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  1609→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
  1610→+
  1611→+  @authorization_action :read
  1612→+  @authorization_category :bundle
  1613→+
  1614→+  schema do
  1615→+    field :account_handle, :string,
  1616→+      required: true,
  1617→+      description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
  1618→+
  1619→+    field :project_handle, :string,
  1620→+      required: true,
  1621→+      description: "The project handle."
  1622→+
  1623→+    field :git_branch, :string, description: "Filter by git branch."
  1624→+    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
  1625→+    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
  1626→+  end
  1627→+
  1628→+  @impl true
  1629→+  def execute(arguments, frame) do
  1630→+    with {:ok, project} <-
  1631→+           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
  1632→+             arguments,
  1633→+             frame,
  1634→+             @authorization_action,
  1635→+             @authorization_category
  1636→+           ) do
  1637→+      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
  1638→+      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
  1639→+      filters = build_filters(project.id, arguments)
  1640→+
  1641→+      {bundles, meta} =
  1642→+        Bundles.list_bundles(%{
  1643→+          filters: filters,
  1644→+          order_by: [:inserted_at],
  1645→+          order_directions: [:desc],
  1646→+          page: page,
  1647→+          page_size: page_size
  1648→+        })
  1649→+
  1650→+      data = %{
  1651→+        bundles:
  1652→+          Enum.map(bundles, fn bundle ->
  1653→+            %{
  1654→+              id: bundle.id,
  1655→+              name: bundle.name,
  1656→+              app_bundle_id: bundle.app_bundle_id,
  1657→+              version: bundle.version,
  1658→+              type: to_string(bundle.type),
  1659→+              supported_platforms: bundle.supported_platforms || [],
  1660→+              install_size: bundle.install_size,
  1661→+              download_size: bundle.download_size,
  1662→+              git_branch: bundle.git_branch,
  1663→+              git_commit_sha: bundle.git_commit_sha,
  1664→+              inserted_at: Formatter.iso8601(bundle.inserted_at)
  1665→+            }
  1666→+          end),
  1667→+        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
  1668→+      }
  1669→+
  1670→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  1671→+    end
  1672→+  end
  1673→+
  1674→+  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
  1675→+    base = [%{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id}]
  1676→+
  1677→+    case Map.get(arguments, :git_branch) do
  1678→+      nil -> base
  1679→+      value -> base ++ [%{field: :git_branch, op: :==, value: value}]
  1680→+    end
  1681→+  end
  1682→+end

  1689→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListCacheRuns do
  1690→+  @moduledoc """
  1691→+  List cache runs for a project. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}.
  1692→+  """
  1693→+
  1694→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  1695→+
  1696→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  1697→+  alias Tuist.CommandEvents
  1698→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  1699→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
  1700→+
  1701→+  @authorization_action :read
  1702→+  @authorization_category :run
  1703→+
  1704→+  schema do
  1705→+    field :account_handle, :string,
  1706→+      required: true,
  1707→+      description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
  1708→+
  1709→+    field :project_handle, :string,
  1710→+      required: true,
  1711→+      description: "The project handle."
  1712→+
  1713→+    field :git_branch, :string, description: "Filter by git branch."
  1714→+    field :git_commit_sha, :string, description: "Filter by git commit SHA."
  1715→+    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
  1716→+    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
  1717→+  end
  1718→+
  1719→+  @impl true
  1720→+  def execute(arguments, frame) do
  1721→+    with {:ok, project} <-
  1722→+           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
  1723→+             arguments,
  1724→+             frame,
  1725→+             @authorization_action,
  1726→+             @authorization_category
  1727→+           ) do
  1728→+      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
  1729→+      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
  1730→+      filters = build_filters(project.id, arguments)
  1731→+
  1732→+      {events, meta} =
  1733→+        CommandEvents.list_command_events(%{
  1734→+          filters: filters,
  1735→+          order_by: [:ran_at],
  1736→+          order_directions: [:desc],
  1737→+          page: page,
  1738→+          page_size: page_size
  1739→+        })
  1740→+
  1741→+      data = %{
  1742→+        cache_runs:
  1743→+          Enum.map(events, fn event ->
  1744→+            %{
  1745→+              id: event.id,
  1746→+              duration: event.duration,
  1747→+              status: status_to_string(event.status),
  1748→+              tuist_version: event.tuist_version,
  1749→+              swift_version: event.swift_version,
  1750→+              macos_version: event.macos_version,
  1751→+              is_ci: event.is_ci,
  1752→+              git_branch: event.git_branch,
  1753→+              git_commit_sha: event.git_commit_sha,
  1754→+              git_ref: event.git_ref,
  1755→+              command_arguments: event.command_arguments,
  1756→+              cacheable_targets: event.cacheable_targets,
  1757→+              local_cache_target_hits: event.local_cache_target_hits,
  1758→+              remote_cache_target_hits: event.remote_cache_target_hits,
  1759→+              ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(event.created_at, naive: :utc)
  1760→+            }
  1761→+          end),
  1762→+        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
  1763→+      }
  1764→+
  1765→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  1766→+    end
  1767→+  end
  1768→+
  1769→+  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
  1770→+    base = [
  1771→+      %{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id},
  1772→+      %{field: :name, op: :==, value: "cache"}
  1773→+    ]
  1774→+
  1775→+    Enum.reduce([:git_branch, :git_commit_sha], base, fn field, filters ->
  1776→+      case Map.get(arguments, field) do
  1777→+        nil -> filters
  1778→+        value -> filters ++ [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value}]
  1779→+      end
  1780→+    end)
  1781→+  end
  1782→+
  1783→+  defp status_to_string(0), do: "success"
  1784→+  defp status_to_string(1), do: "failure"
  1785→+  defp status_to_string(nil), do: "success"
  1786→+  defp status_to_string(status) when is_atom(status), do: Atom.to_string(status)
  1787→+  defp status_to_string(status), do: to_string(status)
  1788→+end

  1838→+      {events, meta} =
  1839→+        CommandEvents.list_command_events(%{
  1840→+          filters: filters,
  1841→+          order_by: [:ran_at],
  1842→+          order_directions: [:desc],
  1843→+          page: page,
  1844→+          page_size: page_size
  1845→+        })
  1846→+
  1847→+      data = %{
  1848→+        generations:
  1849→+          Enum.map(events, fn event ->
  1850→+            %{
  1851→+              id: event.id,
  1852→+              duration: event.duration,
  1853→+              status: status_to_string(event.status),
  1854→+              tuist_version: event.tuist_version,
  1855→+              swift_version: event.swift_version,
  1856→+              macos_version: event.macos_version,
  1857→+              is_ci: event.is_ci,
  1858→+              git_branch: event.git_branch,
  1859→+              git_commit_sha: event.git_commit_sha,
  1860→+              git_ref: event.git_ref,
  1861→+              command_arguments: event.command_arguments,
  1862→+              cacheable_targets: event.cacheable_targets,
  1863→+              local_cache_target_hits: event.local_cache_target_hits,
  1864→+              remote_cache_target_hits: event.remote_cache_target_hits,
  1865→+              ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(event.created_at, naive: :utc)
  1866→+            }
  1867→+          end),
  1868→+        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
  1869→+      }
  1870→+
  1871→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  1872→+    end
  1873→+  end
  1874→+
  1875→+  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
  1876→+    base = [
  1877→+      %{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id},
  1878→+      %{field: :name, op: :==, value: "generate"}
  1879→+    ]
  1880→+
  1881→+    Enum.reduce([:git_branch, :git_commit_sha], base, fn field, filters ->
  1882→+      case Map.get(arguments, field) do

  1893→+  defp status_to_string(status), do: to_string(status)
  1894→+end

  1916→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCaseRunAttachments do
  1917→+  @moduledoc """
  1918→+  List attachments for a test case run. Each attachment includes a temporary download URL (valid for 1 hour).
  1919→+  """
  1920→+
  1921→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  1922→+
  1923→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  1924→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  1925→+  alias Tuist.Storage
  1926→+  alias Tuist.Tests
  1927→+
  1928→+  @authorization_action :read
  1929→+  @authorization_category :test
  1930→+
  1931→+  schema do
  1932→+    field :test_case_run_id, :string,
  1933→+      required: true,
  1934→+      description: "The ID of the test case run."
  1935→+  end
  1936→+
  1937→+  @impl true
  1938→+  def execute(%{test_case_run_id: test_case_run_id}, frame) do
  1939→+    with {:ok, run} <-
  1940→+           ToolSupport.load_resource(
  1941→+             Tests.get_test_case_run_by_id(test_case_run_id, preload: [:attachments]),
  1942→+             "Test case run not found: #{test_case_run_id}",
  1943→+             frame
  1944→+           ),
  1945→+         {:ok, project} <-
  1946→+           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
  1947→+             frame,
  1948→+             run.project_id,
  1949→+             @authorization_action,
  1950→+             @authorization_category
  1951→+           ) do
  1952→+      data = %{
  1953→+        test_case_run_id: test_case_run_id,
  1954→+        attachments:
  1955→+          Enum.map(run.attachments || [], fn attachment ->
  1956→+            s3_object_key =
  1957→+              Tests.attachment_storage_key(%{
  1958→+                account_handle: project.account.name,
  1959→+                project_handle: project.name,
  1960→+                test_case_run_id: test_case_run_id,
  1961→+                attachment_id: attachment.id,
  1962→+                file_name: attachment.file_name
  1963→+              })
  1964→+
  1965→+            %{
  1966→+              id: attachment.id,
  1967→+              file_name: attachment.file_name,
  1968→+              type: attachment_type(attachment.file_name),
  1969→+              download_url: Storage.generate_download_url(s3_object_key, project.account, expires_in: 3600)
  1970→+            }
  1971→+          end)
  1972→+      }
  1973→+
  1974→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  1975→+    end
  1976→+  end
  1977→+
  1978→+  defp attachment_type(file_name) do
  1979→+    ext = file_name |> String.downcase() |> Path.extname()
  1980→+
  1981→+    cond do
  1982→+      ext in [".png", ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".gif", ".webp", ".heic"] -> "image"
  1983→+      ext in [".txt"] -> "text"
  1984→+      ext in [".log"] -> "log"
  1985→+      ext in [".json"] -> "json"
  1986→+      ext in [".xml"] -> "xml"
  1987→+      ext in [".csv"] -> "csv"
  1988→+      ext in [".ips"] -> "crash_report"
  1989→+      true -> "file"
  1990→+    end
  1991→+  end
  1992→+end

  2078→+  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
  2079→+    base = [%{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id}]

  2081→+    base =

  2087→+    base =

  2279→+    field :scheme, :string, description: "Filter by scheme name."
  2280→+    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."

  2297→+      {runs, meta} =

  2306→+      metrics_map =

  2355→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestSuiteRuns do
  2356→+  @moduledoc """
  2357→+  List test suite runs for a specific test run, optionally filtered by module. The test_run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/tests/test-runs/{id}.
  2358→+  """
  2359→+
  2360→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  2361→+
  2362→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  2363→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  2364→+  alias Tuist.Tests
  2365→+
  2366→+  @authorization_action :read
  2367→+  @authorization_category :test
  2368→+
  2369→+  schema do
  2370→+    field :test_run_id, :string,
  2371→+      required: true,
  2372→+      description: "The ID of the test run."
  2373→+
  2374→+    field :module_name, :string, description: "Filter suites by module name."
  2375→+    field :status, :string, description: "Filter by status: success, failure, or skipped."
  2376→+    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
  2377→+    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
  2378→+  end
  2379→+
  2380→+  @impl true
  2381→+  def execute(%{test_run_id: test_run_id} = arguments, frame) do
  2382→+    with {:ok, run} <-
  2383→+           ToolSupport.load_resource(
  2384→+             Tests.get_test(test_run_id),
  2385→+             "Test run not found: #{test_run_id}",
  2386→+             frame
  2387→+           ),
  2388→+         {:ok, _project} <-
  2389→+           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
  2390→+             frame,
  2391→+             run.project_id,
  2392→+             @authorization_action,
  2393→+             @authorization_category
  2394→+           ) do
  2395→+      filters = [%{field: :test_run_id, op: :==, value: test_run_id}]
  2396→+
  2397→+      filters =
  2398→+        Enum.reduce([:status], filters, fn field, acc ->
  2399→+          case Map.get(arguments, field) do
  2400→+            nil -> acc
  2401→+            value -> acc ++ [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value}]
  2402→+          end
  2403→+        end)
  2404→+
  2405→+      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
  2406→+      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
  2407→+
  2408→+      {suites, meta} =
  2409→+        Tests.list_test_suite_runs(%{
  2410→+          filters: filters,
  2411→+          order_by: [:duration],
  2412→+          order_directions: [:desc],
  2413→+          page: page,
  2414→+          page_size: page_size
  2415→+        })
  2416→+
  2417→+      suites =
  2418→+        case Map.get(arguments, :module_name) do
  2419→+          nil ->
  2420→+            suites
  2421→+
  2422→+          module_name ->
  2423→+            module_ids = get_module_run_ids(test_run_id, module_name)
  2424→+            Enum.filter(suites, &(&1.test_module_run_id in module_ids))
  2425→+        end
  2426→+
  2427→+      data = %{
  2428→+        suites:
  2429→+          Enum.map(suites, fn suite ->
  2430→+            %{
  2431→+              name: suite.name,
  2432→+              status: to_string(suite.status),
  2433→+              is_flaky: suite.is_flaky,
  2434→+              duration: suite.duration,
  2435→+              test_case_count: suite.test_case_count,
  2436→+              avg_test_case_duration: suite.avg_test_case_duration
  2437→+            }
  2438→+          end),
  2439→+        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
  2440→+      }
  2441→+
  2442→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  2443→+    end
  2444→+  end
  2445→+
  2446→+  defp get_module_run_ids(test_run_id, module_name) do
  2447→+    {modules, _meta} =
  2448→+      Tests.list_test_module_runs(%{
  2449→+        filters: [
  2450→+          %{field: :test_run_id, op: :==, value: test_run_id},
  2451→+          %{field: :name, op: :==, value: module_name}
  2452→+        ],
  2453→+        page: 1,
  2454→+        page_size: 10
  2455→+      })
  2456→+
  2457→+    Enum.map(modules, & &1.id)
  2458→+  end
  2459→+end

  2758→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuildIssues do
  2759→+  @moduledoc """
  2760→+  List build issues (warnings and errors) for a specific build run. The build_run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/builds/build-runs/{id}.
  2761→+  """
  2762→+
  2763→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  2764→+
  2765→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  2766→+  alias Tuist.Builds
  2767→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  2768→+
  2769→+  @authorization_action :read
  2770→+  @authorization_category :build
  2771→+
  2772→+  schema do
  2773→+    field :build_run_id, :string,
  2774→+      required: true,
  2775→+      description: "The ID of the build run."
  2776→+
  2777→+    field :type, :string, description: "Filter by issue type: warning or error."
  2778→+    field :target, :string, description: "Filter by target name."
  2779→+    field :step_type, :string, description: "Filter by compilation step type (e.g. swift_compilation, linker)."
  2780→+  end
  2781→+
  2782→+  @impl true
  2783→+  def execute(%{build_run_id: build_run_id} = arguments, frame) do
  2784→+    with {:ok, build} <-
  2785→+           ToolSupport.load_resource(
  2786→+             get_build(build_run_id),
  2787→+             "Build not found: #{build_run_id}",
  2788→+             frame
  2789→+           ),
  2790→+         {:ok, _project} <-
  2791→+           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
  2792→+             frame,
  2793→+             build.project_id,
  2794→+             @authorization_action,
  2795→+             @authorization_category
  2796→+           ) do
  2797→+      issues = Builds.list_build_issues(build_run_id)
  2798→+
  2799→+      issues =
  2800→+        issues
  2801→+        |> maybe_filter(:type, Map.get(arguments, :type))
  2802→+        |> maybe_filter(:target, Map.get(arguments, :target))
  2803→+        |> maybe_filter(:step_type, Map.get(arguments, :step_type))
  2804→+
  2805→+      data =
  2806→+        Enum.map(issues, fn issue ->
  2807→+          %{
  2808→+            type: to_string(issue.type),
  2809→+            target: issue.target,
  2810→+            project: issue.project,
  2811→+            title: issue.title,
  2812→+            message: issue.message,
  2813→+            signature: issue.signature,
  2814→+            step_type: to_string(issue.step_type),
  2815→+            path: issue.path,
  2816→+            starting_line: issue.starting_line,
  2817→+            ending_line: issue.ending_line,
  2818→+            starting_column: issue.starting_column,
  2819→+            ending_column: issue.ending_column
  2820→+          }
  2821→+        end)
  2822→+
  2823→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  2824→+    end
  2825→+  end
  2826→+
  2827→+  defp get_build(id) do
  2828→+    case Builds.get_build(id) do
  2829→+      nil -> {:error, :not_found}
  2830→+      build -> {:ok, build}
  2831→+    end
  2832→+  end
  2833→+
  2834→+  defp maybe_filter(items, _field, nil), do: items
  2835→+
  2836→+  defp maybe_filter(items, field, value) do
  2837→+    Enum.filter(items, fn item -> to_string(Map.get(item, field)) == value end)

  2839→+end

  2846→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuildTargets do
  2847→+  @moduledoc """
  2848→+  List build targets for a specific Xcode build run. Only available for projects with build_system=xcode. The project is derived from the build run, so no account or project handle is needed. The build_run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/builds/build-runs/{id}.
  2849→+  """
  2850→+
  2851→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  2852→+
  2853→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  2854→+  alias Tuist.Builds
  2855→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  2856→+
  2857→+  @authorization_action :read
  2858→+  @authorization_category :build
  2859→+
  2860→+  schema do
  2861→+    field :build_run_id, :string,
  2862→+      required: true,
  2863→+      description: "The ID of the build run."
  2864→+
  2865→+    field :status, :string, description: "Filter by target status: success or failure."
  2866→+    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
  2867→+    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
  2868→+  end
  2869→+
  2870→+  @impl true
  2871→+  def execute(%{build_run_id: build_run_id} = arguments, frame) do
  2872→+    with {:ok, build} <-
  2873→+           ToolSupport.load_resource(
  2874→+             get_build(build_run_id),
  2875→+             "Build not found: #{build_run_id}",
  2876→+             frame
  2877→+           ),
  2878→+         {:ok, _project} <-
  2879→+           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
  2880→+             frame,
  2881→+             build.project_id,
  2882→+             @authorization_action,
  2883→+             @authorization_category
  2884→+           ) do
  2885→+      filters = [%{field: :build_run_id, op: :==, value: build_run_id}]
  2886→+
  2887→+      filters =
  2888→+        case Map.get(arguments, :status) do
  2889→+          nil -> filters
  2890→+          status -> filters ++ [%{field: :status, op: :==, value: status}]
  2891→+        end
  2892→+
  2893→+      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
  2894→+      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
  2895→+
  2896→+      {targets, meta} =
  2897→+        Builds.list_build_targets(%{
  2898→+          filters: filters,
  2899→+          order_by: [:build_duration],
  2900→+          order_directions: [:desc],
  2901→+          page: page,
  2902→+          page_size: page_size
  2903→+        })
  2904→+
  2905→+      data = %{
  2906→+        targets:
  2907→+          Enum.map(targets, fn target ->
  2908→+            %{
  2909→+              name: target.name,
  2910→+              project: target.project,
  2911→+              build_duration: target.build_duration,
  2912→+              compilation_duration: target.compilation_duration,
  2913→+              status: to_string(target.status)
  2914→+            }
  2915→+          end),
  2916→+        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
  2917→+      }
  2918→+
  2919→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  2920→+    end
  2921→+  end
  2922→+
  2923→+  defp get_build(id) do
  2924→+    case Builds.get_build(id) do
  2925→+      nil -> {:error, :not_found}
  2926→+      build -> {:ok, build}
  2927→+    end
  2928→+  end
  2929→+end

  2936→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuilds do
  2937→+  @moduledoc """
  2938→+  List Xcode build runs for a project. Only available for projects with build_system=xcode. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}.
  2939→+  """
  2940→+
  2941→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  2942→+
  2943→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  2944→+  alias Tuist.Builds
  2945→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  2946→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
  2947→+
  2948→+  @authorization_action :read
  2949→+  @authorization_category :build
  2950→+
  2951→+  schema do
  2952→+    field :account_handle, :string,
  2953→+      required: true,
  2954→+      description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
  2955→+
  2956→+    field :project_handle, :string,
  2957→+      required: true,
  2958→+      description: "The project handle."
  2959→+
  2960→+    field :git_branch, :string, description: "Filter by git branch."
  2961→+    field :status, :string, description: "Filter by status: success or failure."
  2962→+    field :scheme, :string, description: "Filter by scheme name."
  2963→+    field :configuration, :string, description: "Filter by configuration name."
  2964→+    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
  2965→+    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
  2966→+  end
  2967→+
  2968→+  @impl true
  2969→+  def execute(arguments, frame) do
  2970→+    with {:ok, project} <-
  2971→+           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
  2972→+             arguments,
  2973→+             frame,
  2974→+             @authorization_action,
  2975→+             @authorization_category
  2976→+           ) do
  2977→+      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
  2978→+      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
  2979→+      filters = build_filters(project.id, arguments)
  2980→+
  2981→+      {builds, meta} =
  2982→+        Builds.list_build_runs(%{
  2983→+          filters: filters,
  2984→+          order_by: [:inserted_at],
  2985→+          order_directions: [:desc],
  2986→+          page: page,
  2987→+          page_size: page_size
  2988→+        })
  2989→+
  2990→+      data = %{
  2991→+        builds:
  2992→+          Enum.map(builds, fn build ->
  2993→+            %{
  2994→+              id: build.id,
  2995→+              duration: build.duration,
  2996→+              status: to_string(build.status),
  2997→+              category: if(build.category != "", do: build.category),
  2998→+              scheme: build.scheme,
  2999→+              configuration: build.configuration,

3000→+              is_ci: build.is_ci,
  3001→+              git_branch: build.git_branch,
  3002→+              git_commit_sha: build.git_commit_sha,
  3003→+              cacheable_tasks_count: build.cacheable_tasks_count,
  3004→+              cacheable_task_local_hits_count: build.cacheable_task_local_hits_count,
  3005→+              cacheable_task_remote_hits_count: build.cacheable_task_remote_hits_count,
  3006→+              inserted_at: Formatter.iso8601(build.inserted_at, naive: :utc)
  3007→+            }
  3008→+          end),
  3009→+        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
  3010→+      }
  3011→+
  3012→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  3013→+    end
  3014→+  end
  3015→+
  3016→+  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
  3017→+    base = [%{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id}]
  3018→+
  3019→+    Enum.reduce([:git_branch, :status, :scheme, :configuration], base, fn field, filters ->
  3020→+      case Map.get(arguments, field) do
  3021→+        nil -> filters
  3022→+        value -> filters ++ [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value}]
  3023→+      end
  3024→+    end)
  3025→+  end
  3026→+end

  3033→+defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeModuleCacheTargets do
  3034→+  @moduledoc """
  3035→+  List module cache targets for a generation or cache run, showing per-target cache hit/miss status and subhashes. Only available for projects with build_system=xcode. The run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/runs/{id}.
  3036→+  """
  3037→+
  3038→+  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
  3039→+
  3040→+  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  3041→+  alias Tuist.CommandEvents
  3042→+  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
  3043→+  alias Tuist.Xcode
  3044→+
  3045→+  @authorization_action :read
  3046→+  @authorization_category :run
  3047→+
  3048→+  schema do
  3049→+    field :run_id, :string,
  3050→+      required: true,
  3051→+      description: "The ID of the generation or cache run."
  3052→+
  3053→+    field :cache_status, :string, description: "Filter by cache status: miss, local, or remote."
  3054→+
  3055→+    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
  3056→+    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
  3057→+  end
  3058→+
  3059→+  @impl true
  3060→+  def execute(%{run_id: run_id} = arguments, frame) do
  3061→+    with {:ok, event} <-
  3062→+           ToolSupport.load_resource(
  3063→+             get_command_event(run_id),
  3064→+             "Run not found: #{run_id}",
  3065→+             frame
  3066→+           ),
  3067→+         {:ok, _project} <-
  3068→+           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
  3069→+             frame,
  3070→+             event.project_id,
  3071→+             @authorization_action,
  3072→+             @authorization_category
  3073→+           ) do
  3074→+      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
  3075→+      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
  3076→+
  3077→+      flop_params = maybe_add_filter(%{page: page, page_size: page_size}, arguments)
  3078→+
  3079→+      {analytics, meta} = Xcode.binary_cache_analytics(event, flop_params)
  3080→+
  3081→+      data = %{
  3082→+        targets:
  3083→+          Enum.map(analytics.cacheable_targets, fn target ->
  3084→+            %{
  3085→+              name: target.name,
  3086→+              cache_status: to_string(target.binary_cache_hit),
  3087→+              cache_hash: target.binary_cache_hash,
  3088→+              product: non_empty(target.product),
  3089→+              bundle_id: non_empty(target.bundle_id),
  3090→+              product_name: non_empty(target.product_name),
  3091→+              subhashes: build_subhashes(target)
  3092→+            }
  3093→+          end),
  3094→+        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
  3095→+      }
  3096→+
  3097→+      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
  3098→+    end
  3099→+  end
  3100→+
  3101→+  defp get_command_event(id) do
  3102→+    case CommandEvents.get_command_event_by_id(id) do
  3103→+      {:ok, event} when event.name in ["generate", "cache"] -> {:ok, event}
  3104→+      {:ok, _event} -> {:error, :not_found}
  3105→+      error -> error
  3106→+    end
  3107→+  end
  3108→+
  3109→+  defp maybe_add_filter(flop_params, arguments) do
  3110→+    case Map.get(arguments, :cache_status) do
  3111→+      nil ->
  3112→+        flop_params
  3113→+
  3114→+      status ->
  3115→+        Map.put(flop_params, :filters, [
  3116→+          %{field: :binary_cache_hit, op: :==, value: status}
  3117→+        ])
  3118→+    end
  3119→+  end
  3120→+
  3121→+  defp non_empty(""), do: nil
  3122→+  defp non_empty(value), do: value
  3123→+
  3124→+  defp build_subhashes(target) do
  3125→+    %{
  3126→+      sources: non_empty(target.sources_hash),
  3127→+      resources: non_empty(target.resources_hash),
  3128→+      copy_files: non_empty(target.copy_files_hash),
  3129→+      core_data_models: non_empty(target.core_data_models_hash),
  3130→+      target_scripts: non_empty(target.target_scripts_hash),
  3131→+      environment: non_empty(target.environment_hash),
  3132→+      headers: non_empty(target.headers_hash),
  3133→+      deployment_target: non_empty(target.deployment_target_hash),
  3134→+      info_plist: non_empty(target.info_plist_hash),
  3135→+      entitlements: non_empty(target.entitlements_hash),
  3136→+      dependencies: non_empty(target.dependencies_hash),
  3137→+      project_settings: non_empty(target.project_settings_hash),
  3138→+      target_settings: non_empty(target.target_settings_hash),
  3139→+      buildable_folders: non_empty(target.buildable_folders_hash),
  3140→+      external: non_empty(target.external_hash)
  3141→+    }
  3142→+    |> Enum.reject(fn {_k, v} -> is_nil(v) end)
  3143→+    |> Map.new()
  3144→+  end
  3145→+end

  3264→+  describe "fix_flaky_test" do
  3265→+    test "returns prompt messages" do
  3266→+      request = %{
  3267→+        "method" => "prompts/get",
  3268→+        "params" => %{
  3269→+          "name" => "fix_flaky_test",
  3270→+          "arguments" => %{"test_case_id" => "abc-123"}

  3273→+
  3274→+      assert {:reply, %{"messages" => messages}, _frame} = Server.handle_request(request, Frame.new())
  3275→+
  3276→+      assert length(messages) == 1

content.text:
Here is a summary of PR #9732.

## PR Summary: "feat(server): add comparison primitives for MCP, API, and agent skills"

This PR adds a large set of MCP tools and prompts to the Tuist server, enabling AI agents to fetch, inspect, and compare various Tuist data structures (builds, tests, bundles, generations, cache runs). It follows an [RFC published on the Tuist community forum](https://community.tuist.dev/t/rfc-comparison-primitives-for-mcp-cli-and-agent-skills/925).

### New MCP Tools (22 new tools total)

**Xcode Build tools (7):**
- `list_xcode_builds` -- List Xcode build runs for a project (filterable by branch, status, scheme, configuration)
- `get_xcode_build` -- Get detailed metrics for a single build run
- `list_xcode_build_targets` -- List per-target build/compilation durations
- `list_xcode_build_files` -- List per-file compilation durations (sorted slowest-first by default)
- `list_xcode_build_issues` -- List warnings and errors
- `list_xcode_build_cache_tasks` -- List cache hit/miss status per task
- `list_xcode_build_cas_outputs` -- List CAS upload/download operations

**Test tools (6):**
- `list_test_runs` -- List test runs for a project (filterable by branch, status, scheme)
- `list_test_module_runs` -- List per-module results within a test run
- `list_test_suite_runs` -- List per-suite results (optionally filtered by module)
- `list_test_case_runs` -- List individual test case runs (filterable by test_case_id, test_run_id, flaky flag)
- `list_test_case_run_attachments` -- List attachments (screenshots, logs, crash reports) with temporary download URLs

**Bundle tools (3):**
- `list_bundles` -- List bundles (app binaries) for a project
- `get_bundle` -- Get bundle metadata (sizes, version, platforms)
- `get_bundle_artifact_tree` -- Get the full artifact tree as a flat list sorted by path

**Generation & Cache tools (5):**
- `list_generations` -- List generation runs (filtered to `name == "generate"`)
- `get_generation` -- Get detailed generation info
- `list_cache_runs` -- List cache runs (filtered to `name == "cache"`)
- `get_cache_run` -- Get detailed cache run info
- `list_xcode_module_cache_targets` -- List per-target module cache hit/miss status and subhashes (works for both generation and cache runs)

### New MCP Prompts (6 new comparison prompts)

Each prompt generates a structured workflow that guides an AI agent through fetching data, comparing base vs. head, and producing a summary with actionable recommendations:

- **`compare_builds`** -- Compare two Xcode (or Gradle) build runs. Adapts the workflow and tool references based on the project's `build_system`. Covers duration regressions, cache changes, target-level drill-downs, file-level analysis, and new issues.
- **`compare_test_runs`** -- Compare two test runs to identify new failures, newly flaky tests, fixed tests, and duration regressions. Guides through module-level, suite-level, and individual test case inspection including attachments.
- **`compare_bundles`** -- Compare two bundles by install/download size and artifact tree diff. Identifies new, removed, and size-changed artifacts.
- **`compare_test_case`** -- Compare a single test case's behavior across two branches, analyzing pass/fail status, duration, flakiness, and attachments.
- **`compare_generations`** -- Compare two `tuist generate` runs, focusing on cache hit rate changes and root-cause analysis of cache invalidation through subhash diffing.
- **`compare_cache_runs`** -- Compare two `tuist cache` runs with similar root-cause analysis. Both generation and cache comparison prompts include detailed guidance on identifying the "root cause target" when many targets are invalidated via dependency hash cascading.

### Existing Prompt Enhancement

- **`fix_flaky_test`** -- Updated to include a new "Inspect attachments" step (step 5) that uses `list_test_case_run_attachments` for diagnostic artifacts.

### Shared Infrastructure Changes

- **`PromptSupport`** (new module) -- Shared helpers for prompts: `resolve_project_handles/1`, `resolve_project_metadata/2`, `resolve_default_branch/2`. Used by all comparison prompts to look up the project's default branch and build system.
- **`ToolSupport`** (extended) -- Added `resolve_and_authorize_project/4` (consolidates handle-based project lookup + authorization), `page/1`, `page_size/1`, `pagination_metadata/1` helpers. Existing tools like `ListTestCases` and `GetTestCase` were refactored to use these shared helpers.
- **`list_projects`** tool -- Now returns `build_system` and `default_branch` fields.
- **Domain layer** -- `Tuist.Builds.list_build_issues/1` and `Tuist.Bundles.get_bundle_artifact_tree/1` added as new query functions.

### Tests

The previously monolithic `server_test.exs` was split into per-domain test files:
- `prompts_test.exs` -- Tests all 7 prompts
- `bundle_tools_test.exs` -- Tests bundle tools
- `generation_and_cache_tools_test.exs` -- Tests generation, cache, and module cache tools
- `test_tools_test.exs` -- Tests all test-related tools
- `xcode_build_tools_test.exs` -- Tests all Xcode build tools

Gradle-related tools/prompts, skills, CLI commands, and API endpoints were explicitly left out of scope.

   - Check how commands are organized (e.g., subcommands under groups)

2. Look at the existing agent skills:
   - skills/ directory structure
   - Read a representative skill file to understand the format
   - Check if there's a skill that already interacts with MCP tools (like the fix-flaky-tests skill)

3. Check the server's OpenAPI spec or router to understand what API endpoints already exist:
   - server/lib/tuist_web/router.ex or similar
   - Any existing API endpoints for builds, tests, bundles, generations, cache runs

4. Check if there are existing CLI commands for things like `tuist builds list`, `tuist test-runs list`, etc.

Provide a detailed summary of:
- Existing CLI command structure and patterns
- Existing skills structure and format
- What API endpoints already exist
- What's missing that would need to be created
