1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuilds do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  List Xcode build runs for a project. Only available for projects with build_system=xcode. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.Builds
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    11→  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :build
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :account_handle, :string,
    18→      required: true,
    19→      description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    20→
    21→    field :project_handle, :string,
    22→      required: true,
    23→      description: "The project handle."
    24→
    25→    field :git_branch, :string, description: "Filter by git branch."
    26→    field :status, :string, description: "Filter by status: success or failure."
    27→    field :scheme, :string, description: "Filter by scheme name."
    28→    field :configuration, :string, description: "Filter by configuration name."
    29→    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
    30→    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
    31→  end
    32→
    33→  @impl true
    34→  def execute(arguments, frame) do
    35→    with {:ok, project} <-
    36→           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
    37→             arguments,
    38→             frame,
    39→             @authorization_action,
    40→             @authorization_category
    41→           ) do
    42→      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
    43→      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
    44→      filters = build_filters(project.id, arguments)
    45→
    46→      {builds, meta} =
    47→        Builds.list_build_runs(%{
    48→          filters: filters,
    49→          order_by: [:inserted_at],
    50→          order_directions: [:desc],
    51→          page: page,
    52→          page_size: page_size
    53→        })
    54→
    55→      data = %{
    56→        builds:
    57→          Enum.map(builds, fn build ->
    58→            %{
    59→              id: build.id,
    60→              duration: build.duration,
    61→              status: to_string(build.status),
    62→              category: if(build.category != "", do: build.category),
    63→              scheme: build.scheme,
    64→              configuration: build.configuration,
    65→              is_ci: build.is_ci,
    66→              git_branch: build.git_branch,
    67→              git_commit_sha: build.git_commit_sha,
    68→              cacheable_tasks_count: build.cacheable_tasks_count,
    69→              cacheable_task_local_hits_count: build.cacheable_task_local_hits_count,
    70→              cacheable_task_remote_hits_count: build.cacheable_task_remote_hits_count,
    71→              inserted_at: Formatter.iso8601(build.inserted_at, naive: :utc)
    72→            }
    73→          end),
    74→        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
    75→      }
    76→
    77→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    78→    end
    79→  end
    80→
    81→  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
    82→    base = [%{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id}]
    83→
    84→    Enum.reduce([:git_branch, :status, :scheme, :configuration], base, fn field, filters ->
    85→      case Map.get(arguments, field) do
    86→        nil -> filters
    87→        value -> filters ++ [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value}]
    88→      end
    89→    end)
    90→  end
    91→end
    92→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetXcodeBuild do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Get detailed information about a specific build run. The build_run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/builds/build-runs/{id}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.Builds
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    11→  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :build
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :build_run_id, :string,
    18→      required: true,
    19→      description: "The ID of the build run."
    20→  end
    21→
    22→  @impl true
    23→  def execute(%{build_run_id: build_run_id}, frame) do
    24→    with {:ok, build} <-
    25→           ToolSupport.load_resource(
    26→             get_build(build_run_id),
    27→             "Build not found: #{build_run_id}",
    28→             frame
    29→           ),
    30→         {:ok, _project} <-
    31→           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
    32→             frame,
    33→             build.project_id,
    34→             @authorization_action,
    35→             @authorization_category
    36→           ) do
    37→      data = %{
    38→        id: build.id,
    39→        duration: build.duration,
    40→        status: to_string(build.status),
    41→        category: if(build.category != "", do: build.category),
    42→        scheme: build.scheme,
    43→        configuration: build.configuration,
    44→        xcode_version: build.xcode_version,
    45→        macos_version: build.macos_version,
    46→        model_identifier: build.model_identifier,
    47→        is_ci: build.is_ci,
    48→        git_branch: build.git_branch,
    49→        git_commit_sha: build.git_commit_sha,
    50→        git_ref: build.git_ref,
    51→        cacheable_tasks_count: build.cacheable_tasks_count,
    52→        cacheable_task_local_hits_count: build.cacheable_task_local_hits_count,
    53→        cacheable_task_remote_hits_count: build.cacheable_task_remote_hits_count,
    54→        inserted_at: Formatter.iso8601(build.inserted_at, naive: :utc)
    55→      }
    56→
    57→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    58→    end
    59→  end
    60→
    61→  defp get_build(id) do
    62→    case Builds.get_build(id) do
    63→      nil -> {:error, :not_found}
    64→      build -> {:ok, build}
    65→    end
    66→  end
    67→end
    68→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestRuns do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  List test runs for a project. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
    11→  alias Tuist.Tests
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :test
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :account_handle, :string,
    18→      required: true,
    19→      description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    20→
    21→    field :project_handle, :string,
    22→      required: true,
    23→      description: "The project handle."
    24→
    25→    field :git_branch, :string, description: "Filter by git branch."
    26→    field :status, :string, description: "Filter by status: success, failure, or skipped."
    27→    field :scheme, :string, description: "Filter by scheme name."
    28→    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
    29→    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
    30→  end
    31→
    32→  @impl true
    33→  def execute(arguments, frame) do
    34→    with {:ok, project} <-
    35→           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
    36→             arguments,
    37→             frame,
    38→             @authorization_action,
    39→             @authorization_category
    40→           ) do
    41→      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
    42→      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
    43→      filters = build_filters(project.id, arguments)
    44→
    45→      {runs, meta} =
    46→        Tests.list_test_runs(%{
    47→          filters: filters,
    48→          order_by: [:ran_at],
    49→          order_directions: [:desc],
    50→          page: page,
    51→          page_size: page_size
    52→        })
    53→
    54→      metrics_map =
    55→        runs
    56→        |> Tests.Analytics.test_runs_metrics()
    57→        |> Map.new(&{&1.test_run_id, &1})
    58→
    59→      data = %{
    60→        test_runs:
    61→          Enum.map(runs, fn run ->
    62→            metrics = Map.get(metrics_map, run.id, %{})
    63→
    64→            %{
    65→              id: run.id,
    66→              duration: run.duration,
    67→              status: to_string(run.status),
    68→              is_ci: run.is_ci,
    69→              is_flaky: run.is_flaky,
    70→              scheme: run.scheme,
    71→              git_branch: run.git_branch,
    72→              git_commit_sha: run.git_commit_sha,
    73→              ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(run.ran_at, naive: :utc),
    74→              total_test_count: Map.get(metrics, :total_tests, 0),
    75→              ran_tests: Map.get(metrics, :ran_tests, 0),
    76→              skipped_tests: Map.get(metrics, :skipped_tests, 0)
    77→            }
    78→          end),
    79→        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
    80→      }
    81→
    82→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    83→    end
    84→  end
    85→
    86→  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
    87→    base = [%{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id}]
    88→
    89→    Enum.reduce([:git_branch, :status, :scheme], base, fn field, filters ->
    90→      case Map.get(arguments, field) do
    91→        nil -> filters
    92→        value -> filters ++ [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value}]
    93→      end
    94→    end)
    95→  end
    96→end
    97→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListBundles do

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListCacheRuns do

    20→
    21→    field :project_handle, :string,
    22→      required: true,
    23→      description: "The project handle."
    24→
    25→    field :git_branch, :string, description: "Filter by git branch."
    26→    field :git_commit_sha, :string, description: "Filter by git commit SHA."
    27→    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
    28→    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
    29→  end
    30→
    31→  @impl true
    32→  def execute(arguments, frame) do
    33→    with {:ok, project} <-
    34→           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
    35→             arguments,
    36→             frame,
    37→             @authorization_action,
    38→             @authorization_category
    39→           ) do
    40→      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
    41→      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
    42→      filters = build_filters(project.id, arguments)
    43→
    44→      {events, meta} =
    45→        CommandEvents.list_command_events(%{
    46→          filters: filters,
    47→          order_by: [:ran_at],
    48→          order_directions: [:desc],
    49→          page: page,
    50→          page_size: page_size
    51→        })
    52→
    53→      data = %{
    54→        cache_runs:
    55→          Enum.map(events, fn event ->
    56→            %{
    57→              id: event.id,
    58→              duration: event.duration,
    59→              status: status_to_string(event.status),
    60→              tuist_version: event.tuist_version,
    61→              swift_version: event.swift_version,
    62→              macos_version: event.macos_version,
    63→              is_ci: event.is_ci,
    64→              git_branch: event.git_branch,
    65→              git_commit_sha: event.git_commit_sha,
    66→              git_ref: event.git_ref,
    67→              command_arguments: event.command_arguments,
    68→              cacheable_targets: event.cacheable_targets,
    69→              local_cache_target_hits: event.local_cache_target_hits,
    70→              remote_cache_target_hits: event.remote_cache_target_hits,
    71→              ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(event.created_at, naive: :utc)
    72→            }
    73→          end),
    74→        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
    75→      }
    76→
    77→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    78→    end
    79→  end
    80→
    81→  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
    82→    base = [
    83→      %{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id},
    84→      %{field: :name, op: :==, value: "cache"}
    85→    ]
    86→
    87→    Enum.reduce([:git_branch, :git_commit_sha], base, fn field, filters ->
    88→      case Map.get(arguments, field) do
    89→        nil -> filters
    90→        value -> filters ++ [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value}]
    91→      end
    92→    end)
    93→  end
    94→
    95→  defp status_to_string(0), do: "success"
    96→  defp status_to_string(1), do: "failure"
    97→  defp status_to_string(nil), do: "success"
    98→  defp status_to_string(status) when is_atom(status), do: Atom.to_string(status)
    99→  defp status_to_string(status), do: to_string(status)
   100→end
   101→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareBuilds do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Guides you through comparing two build runs to identify performance regressions, cache changes, and build issues. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if base or head is a dashboard URL.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport
    10→
    11→  schema do
    12→    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    13→
    14→    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."
    15→
    16→    field :base, :string,
    17→      description: """
    18→      Base build: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. \
    19→      Defaults to the latest build on the project's default branch when omitted.\
    20→      """
    21→
    22→    field :head, :string,
    23→      description: """
    24→      Head build: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. \
    25→      This is the build you want to evaluate. When provided without a base, \
    26→      the base defaults to the latest build on the project's default branch.\
    27→      """
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  @impl true
    31→  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
    32→    base = Map.get(arguments, :base)
    33→    head = Map.get(arguments, :head)
    34→    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
    35→    meta = PromptSupport.resolve_project_metadata(account_handle, project_handle)
    36→
    37→    response =
    38→      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
    39→        "type" => "text",
    40→        "text" => prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, meta)
    41→      })
    42→
    43→    {:reply, response, frame}
    44→  end
    45→
    46→  defp prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, meta) do
    47→    default_branch = meta.default_branch
    48→    build_system = meta.build_system
    49→    resolution = resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch, build_system)
    50→    tools_section = tools_section(build_system)
    51→    workflow_section = workflow_section(build_system)
    52→
    53→    """
    54→    # Compare Builds
    55→
    56→    Use MCP tools to fetch two build runs and compare them to identify regressions and improvements.
    57→
    58→    #{tools_section}
    59→
    60→    ## Workflow
    61→
    62→    #{resolution}
    63→
    64→    #{workflow_section}
    65→
    66→    ### Summary format
    67→
    68→    Produce a structured summary with:
    69→    1. **Overall**: duration change, status change, pass/regress verdict.
    70→    2. **Top regressions**: up to 5 items sorted by absolute time increase.
    71→    3. **Cache changes**: hit-rate delta, notable changes.
    72→    4. **New issues**: new errors and warnings (Xcode only).
    73→    5. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes.
    74→    """
    75→  end
    76→
    77→  defp tools_section(:gradle) do
    78→    """
    79→    ## Available MCP tools
    80→
    81→    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects (includes build_system field).
    82→    - **list_gradle_builds**: List Gradle build runs for a project (supports git_branch filter).
    83→    - **get_gradle_build**: Get detailed metrics for a Gradle build run.
    84→    - **list_gradle_build_tasks**: List Gradle tasks with outcomes and cache status.
    85→    """
    86→  end
    87→
    88→  defp tools_section(_xcode_or_unknown) do
    89→    """
    90→    ## Available MCP tools
    91→
    92→    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects (includes build_system field).
    93→    - **list_xcode_builds**: List Xcode build runs for a project (supports git_branch, status, scheme filters).
    94→    - **get_xcode_build**: Get detailed metrics for an Xcode build run (by ID or dashboard URL).
    95→    - **list_xcode_build_targets**: List per-target build and compilation durations.
    96→    - **list_xcode_build_files**: List per-file compilation durations (sorted slowest-first by default).
    97→    - **list_xcode_build_issues**: List warnings and errors.
    98→    - **list_xcode_build_cache_tasks**: List cache hit/miss status per task.
    99→    - **list_xcode_build_cas_outputs**: List CAS upload/download operations.
   100→    """
   101→  end
   102→
   103→  defp workflow_section(:gradle) do
   104→    """
   105→    ### 2. Compare top-level metrics
   106→
   107→    Compare these fields between the two Gradle builds:
   108→    - **duration**: Total build time. Flag regressions over 10%.
   109→    - **status**: Did either build fail?
   110→    - **tasks_local_hit_count**, **tasks_remote_hit_count**, **tasks_executed_count**: Cache effectiveness changes.
   111→
   112→    ### 3. Drill into tasks (if duration regressed)
   113→
   114→    Use `list_gradle_build_tasks` for both builds. Compare `duration_ms` and `outcome` per task.
   115→    Identify tasks that changed from `local_hit`/`remote_hit` to `executed` (cache misses).
   116→    Sort by absolute time difference.
   117→
   118→    ### 4. Check cache changes
   119→
   120→    Compare task-level cache behavior:
   121→    - Tasks that changed from hit to executed (potential cache invalidation).
   122→    - Tasks that changed from executed to hit (improvements).
   123→    - New tasks that appeared in the head build.
   124→    """
   125→  end
   126→
   127→  defp workflow_section(_xcode_or_unknown) do
   128→    """
   129→    ### 2. Compare top-level metrics
   130→
   131→    Compare these fields between the two Xcode builds:
   132→    - **duration**: Total build time. Flag regressions over 10%.
   133→    - **status**: Did either build fail?
   134→    - **cacheable_tasks_count**, **cacheable_task_local_hits_count**, **cacheable_task_remote_hits_count**: Cache effectiveness changes.
   135→    - **category**: clean vs. incremental (only compare like-for-like).
   136→
   137→    ### 3. Drill into targets (if duration regressed)
   138→
   139→    Use `list_xcode_build_targets` for both builds. Compare `build_duration` and `compilation_duration` per target.
   140→    Identify which targets regressed the most and sort by absolute time difference.
   141→
   142→    ### 4. Drill into files (if a target regressed)
   143→
   144→    Use `list_xcode_build_files` filtered by the regressed target. Compare `compilation_duration` per file.
   145→    Identify the slowest files and any new files that appeared in the head build.
   146→
   147→    ### 5. Check issues
   148→
   149→    Use `list_xcode_build_issues` for both builds. Report:
   150→    - New errors in head that were not in base.
   151→    - New warnings introduced.
   152→    - Issues that were resolved.
   153→
   154→    ### 6. Check cache changes
   155→
   156→    Use `list_xcode_build_cache_tasks` for both builds. Report:
   157→    - Tasks that changed from hit to miss (potential cache invalidation).
   158→    - Tasks that changed from miss to hit (improvements).
   159→    """
   160→  end
   161→
   162→  defp resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch, build_system) do
   163→    branch = default_branch || "main"
   164→    {list_tool, get_tool} = build_tool_names(build_system)
   165→
   166→    project_line =
   167→      if account_handle && project_handle,
   168→        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
   169→        else: ""
   170→
   171→    base_instruction =
   172→      case base do
   173→        nil ->
   174→          "For the **base**, use `#{list_tool}` with `git_branch=#{branch}` and pick the most recent build."
   175→
   176→        _ ->
   177→          "For the **base**, use `#{get_tool}` with `build_run_id=#{base}`."
   178→      end
   179→
   180→    head_instruction =
   181→      case head do
   182→        nil ->
   183→          "For the **head**, detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). " <>
   184→            "If it differs from `#{branch}`, use `#{list_tool}` with that branch and pick the most recent build. " <>
   185→            "If it matches the base branch, ask the user which build to compare."
   186→
   187→        _ ->
   188→          "For the **head**, use `#{get_tool}` with `build_run_id=#{head}`."
   189→      end
   190→
   191→    """
   192→    ### 1. Resolve builds
   193→
   194→    #{project_line}#{base_instruction}
   195→    #{head_instruction}
   196→    """
   197→  end
   198→
   199→  defp build_tool_names(:gradle), do: {"list_gradle_builds", "get_gradle_build"}
   200→  defp build_tool_names(_), do: {"list_xcode_builds", "get_xcode_build"}
   201→end
   202→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareTestRuns do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Guides you through comparing two test runs to identify regressions, new failures, and flaky tests. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if base or head is a dashboard URL.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport
    10→
    11→  schema do
    12→    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    13→
    14→    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."
    15→
    16→    field :base, :string,
    17→      description:
    18→        "Base test run: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. " <>
    19→          "Defaults to the latest test run on the project's default branch when omitted."
    20→
    21→    field :head, :string,
    22→      description:
    23→        "Head test run: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. " <>
    24→          "This is the test run you want to evaluate. When provided without a base, " <>
    25→          "the base defaults to the latest test run on the project's default branch."
    26→  end
    27→
    28→  @impl true
    29→  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
    30→    base = Map.get(arguments, :base)
    31→    head = Map.get(arguments, :head)
    32→    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
    33→    default_branch = PromptSupport.resolve_default_branch(account_handle, project_handle)
    34→
    35→    response =
    36→      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
    37→        "type" => "text",
    38→        "text" => prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
    39→      })
    40→
    41→    {:reply, response, frame}
    42→  end
    43→
    44→  defp prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
    45→    resolution = resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
    46→
    47→    """
    48→    # Compare Test Runs
    49→
    50→    Use MCP tools to fetch two test runs and compare them to identify new failures, flaky tests, and performance changes.
    51→
    52→    ## Available MCP tools
    53→
    54→    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects.
    55→    - **list_test_runs**: List test runs for a project (supports git_branch, status, scheme filters).
    56→    - **get_test_run**: Get detailed metrics for a test run (total/failed/flaky counts, duration).
    57→    - **list_test_module_runs**: List per-module results within a test run.
    58→    - **list_test_suite_runs**: List per-suite results (optionally filtered by module).
    59→    - **list_test_case_runs**: List individual test case runs (supports test_run_id filter).
    60→    - **get_test_case_run**: Get failure details and repetitions for a specific test case run.
    61→    - **list_test_case_run_attachments**: List attachments (screenshots, logs, crash reports) for a test case run. Each attachment includes a temporary download URL.
    62→
    63→    ## Workflow
    64→
    65→    #{resolution}
    66→
    67→    ### 2. Compare top-level metrics
    68→
    69→    Compare these fields between the two test runs:
    70→    - **duration**: Total test duration. Flag regressions over 10%.
    71→    - **total_test_count**: Did the test count change?
    72→    - **failed_test_count**: New failures?
    73→    - **flaky_test_count**: New flaky tests?
    74→    - **status**: Did either run fail entirely?
    75→
    76→    ### 3. Drill into modules (if failures increased)
    77→
    78→    Use `list_test_module_runs` for both runs. Compare `status`, `duration`, and `test_case_count` per module.
    79→    Identify modules that went from passing to failing, or that significantly increased in duration.
    80→
    81→    ### 4. Drill into suites (for regressed modules)
    82→
    83→    Use `list_test_suite_runs` filtered by the regressed module. Compare individual suite results.
    84→
    85→    ### 5. Inspect failing test cases
    86→
    87→    Use `list_test_case_runs` with `test_run_id` for the head run to find failures.
    88→    Use `get_test_case_run` to get failure messages, file paths, and line numbers.
    89→
    90→    Compare with the base run to identify:
    91→    - **New failures**: tests that passed in base but failed in head.
    92→    - **Fixed tests**: tests that failed in base but passed in head.
    93→    - **Persistent failures**: tests that failed in both runs.
    94→
    95→    ### 6. Check for flaky tests
    96→
    97→    Look at tests with `is_flaky: true` in the head run. Use `get_test_case_run` to inspect repetitions.
    98→    Report any newly flaky tests that were stable in the base run.
    99→
   100→    ### 7. Inspect attachments
   101→
   102→    For failing or flaky test case runs, use `list_test_case_run_attachments` to find diagnostic artifacts. Each attachment includes a download URL.
   103→
   104→    - **Text attachments** (logs, crash reports, JSON, XML, CSV): download and include relevant excerpts inline.
   105→    - **Image attachments** (screenshots, PNGs, JPGs): present the download URL so the developer can view them. Describe what the image likely shows based on its file name and context.
   106→
   107→    ### Summary format
   108→
   109→    Produce a structured summary with:
   110→    1. **Overall**: duration change, test count change, pass/fail verdict.
   111→    2. **New failures**: list of tests that newly failed, with failure messages.
   112→    3. **New flaky tests**: tests that became flaky.
   113→    4. **Fixed tests**: tests that were previously failing and now pass.
   114→    5. **Duration regressions**: modules or suites with notable slowdowns.
   115→    6. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes (e.g., "investigate ModuleX failures at path:line", "fix flaky test Y by addressing shared state").
   116→    """
   117→  end
   118→
   119→  defp resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
   120→    branch = default_branch || "main"
   121→
   122→    project_line =
   123→      if account_handle && project_handle,
   124→        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
   125→        else: ""
   126→
   127→    base_instruction =
   128→      case base do
   129→        nil ->
   130→          "For the **base**, use `list_test_runs` with `git_branch=#{branch}` and pick the most recent run."
   131→
   132→        _ ->
   133→          "For the **base**, use `get_test_run` with `test_run_id=#{base}`."
   134→      end
   135→
   136→    head_instruction =
   137→      case head do
   138→        nil ->
   139→          "For the **head**, detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). " <>
   140→            "If it differs from `#{branch}`, use `list_test_runs` with that branch and pick the most recent run. " <>
   141→            "If it matches the base branch, ask the user which test run to compare."
   142→
   143→        _ ->
   144→          "For the **head**, use `get_test_run` with `test_run_id=#{head}`."
   145→      end
   146→
   147→    """
   148→    ### 1. Resolve test runs
   149→
   150→    #{project_line}#{base_instruction}
   151→    #{head_instruction}
   152→    """
   153→  end
   154→end
   155→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareBundles do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Guides you through comparing two bundles to identify size changes across the artifact tree. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if base or head is a dashboard URL.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport
    10→
    11→  schema do
    12→    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    13→
    14→    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."
    15→
    16→    field :base, :string,
    17→      description: """
    18→      Base bundle: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. \
    19→      Defaults to the latest bundle on the project's default branch when omitted.\
    20→      """
    21→
    22→    field :head, :string,
    23→      description: """
    24→      Head bundle: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. \
    25→      This is the bundle you want to evaluate. When provided without a base, \
    26→      the base defaults to the latest bundle on the project's default branch.\
    27→      """
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  @impl true
    31→  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
    32→    base = Map.get(arguments, :base)
    33→    head = Map.get(arguments, :head)
    34→    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
    35→    default_branch = PromptSupport.resolve_default_branch(account_handle, project_handle)
    36→
    37→    response =
    38→      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
    39→        "type" => "text",
    40→        "text" => prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
    41→      })
    42→
    43→    {:reply, response, frame}
    44→  end
    45→
    46→  defp prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
    47→    resolution = resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
    48→
    49→    """
    50→    # Compare Bundles
    51→
    52→    Use MCP tools to fetch two bundles and compare their size and artifact composition.
    53→
    54→    ## Available MCP tools
    55→
    56→    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects.
    57→    - **list_bundles**: List bundles for a project (supports git_branch filter).
    58→    - **get_bundle**: Get bundle metadata (sizes, version, platforms).
    59→    - **get_bundle_artifact_tree**: Get the full artifact tree for a bundle as a flat list sorted by path. Each artifact includes `artifact_type`, `path`, and `size`.
    60→
    61→    ## Workflow
    62→
    63→    #{resolution}
    64→
    65→    ### 2. Compare top-level sizes
    66→
    67→    Compare these fields between the two bundles:
    68→    - **install_size**: The installed size on device. Flag increases over 5%.
    69→    - **download_size**: The download size from the store. Flag increases over 5%.
    70→    - **version**: Note version changes.
    71→    - **supported_platforms**: Note platform changes.
    72→
    73→    ### 3. Compare artifact trees
    74→
    75→    Use `get_bundle_artifact_tree` for both the base and head bundles. Save each result to a local JSON file so you can operate on the data without further API calls.
    76→
    77→    Once you have both artifact trees locally:
    78→    - Match artifacts by `path` between base and head.
    79→    - Calculate size delta for each matched artifact.
    80→    - Identify new artifacts in head that don't exist in base.
    81→    - Identify removed artifacts that were in base but not in head.
    82→    - Sort artifacts by absolute size increase to find the biggest contributors.
    83→
    84→    ### Summary format
    85→
    86→    Produce a structured summary with:
    87→    1. **Overall**: install_size delta, download_size delta, verdict (acceptable/concerning).
    88→    2. **Top size increases**: up to 10 artifacts with the largest size growth.
    89→    3. **New artifacts**: artifacts added in the head bundle.
    90→    4. **Removed artifacts**: artifacts removed from the base bundle.
    91→    5. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes (e.g., "remove unused assets in Resources/", "optimize image at path/to/large.png").
    92→    """
    93→  end
    94→
    95→  defp resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
    96→    branch = default_branch || "main"
    97→
    98→    project_line =
    99→      if account_handle && project_handle,
   100→        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
   101→        else: ""
   102→
   103→    base_instruction =
   104→      case base do
   105→        nil ->
   106→          "For the **base**, use `list_bundles` with `git_branch=#{branch}` and pick the most recent bundle."
   107→
   108→        _ ->
   109→          "For the **base**, use `get_bundle` with `bundle_id=#{base}`."
   110→      end
   111→
   112→    head_instruction =
   113→      case head do
   114→        nil ->
   115→          "For the **head**, detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). " <>
   116→            "If it differs from `#{branch}`, use `list_bundles` with that branch and pick the most recent bundle. " <>
   117→            "If it matches the base branch, ask the user which bundle to compare."
   118→
   119→        _ ->
   120→          "For the **head**, use `get_bundle` with `bundle_id=#{head}`."
   121→      end
   122→
   123→    """
   124→    ### 1. Resolve bundles
   125→
   126→    #{project_line}#{base_instruction}
   127→    #{head_instruction}
   128→    """
   129→  end
   130→end
   131→

1→---
     2→name: fix-flaky-tests
     3→description: Fixes flaky tests by analyzing failure patterns from Tuist test insights, identifying root causes, and applying targeted corrections. Can be invoked with a specific test case URL (e.g. `https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/tests/test-cases/{id}`) or without arguments to discover and fix all flaky tests in the project.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Fix Flaky Tests
     7→
     8→## Quick Start
     9→
    10→You'll typically receive a Tuist test case URL or identifier. Follow these steps to investigate and fix it:
    11→
    12→1. Run `tuist test case show <id-or-identifier> --json` to get reliability metrics for the test.
    13→2. Run `tuist test case run list Module/Suite/TestCase --flaky --json` to see flaky run patterns.
    14→3. Run `tuist test case run show <test-case-run-id> --json` on failing flaky runs to get failure messages and file paths.
    15→4. Read the test source at the reported path and line, identify the flaky pattern, and fix it.
    16→5. Verify by running the test multiple times to confirm it passes consistently.
    17→
    18→If no specific test is provided, start with the Discovery section below.
    19→
    20→## Discovery
    21→
    22→When no specific test case is provided, find all flaky tests in the project:
    23→
    24→```bash
    25→tuist test case list --flaky --json --page-size 50
    26→```
    27→
    28→This returns all test cases currently flagged as flaky. Key fields:
    29→- `module.name` / `suite.name` / `name` — the test identifier
    30→- `avg_duration` — helps prioritize (fix fast unit tests first)
    31→- `is_quarantined` — whether the test is already quarantined
    32→
    33→**Triage strategy:**
    34→1. Group tests by suite — multiple flaky tests in the same suite often share a root cause.
    35→2. Check if failures share a `test_run_id` — tests that all failed in the same run may have been killed by a process crash, not individual test bugs.
    36→3. Look at failure messages to categorize: test logic bugs vs infrastructure issues (network errors, server 502s, conflicts on retry).
    37→
    38→## Investigation
    39→
    40→### 1. Get test case metrics
    41→
    42→You can pass either the UUID or the `Module/Suite/TestCase` identifier:
    43→
    44→```bash
    45→tuist test case show <id> --json
    46→tuist test case show Module/Suite/TestCase --json
    47→```
    48→
    49→Key fields:
    50→- `reliability_rate` — percentage of successful runs (higher is better)
    51→- `flakiness_rate` — percentage of runs marked flaky in the last 30 days
    52→- `total_runs` / `failed_runs` — volume context
    53→- `last_status` — current state
    54→
    55→### 2. View flaky run history
    56→
    57→```bash
    58→tuist test case run list Module/Suite/TestCase --flaky --json
    59→```
    60→
    61→The identifier uses the format `ModuleName/SuiteName/TestCaseName` or `ModuleName/TestCaseName` when there is no suite. This returns only runs that were detected as flaky.
    62→
    63→### 3. View full run history
    64→
    65→```bash
    66→tuist test case run list Module/Suite/TestCase --json --page-size 20
    67→```
    68→
    69→Look for patterns:
    70→- Does it fail on specific branches?
    71→- Does it fail only on CI (`is_ci: true`) or also locally?
    72→- Are failures clustered around specific commits?
    73→
    74→### 4. Get failure details
    75→
    76→```bash
    77→tuist test case run show <test-case-run-id> --json
    78→```
    79→
    80→Key fields:
    81→- `failures[].message` — the assertion or error message
    82→- `failures[].path` — source file path
    83→- `failures[].line_number` — exact line of failure
    84→- `failures[].issue_type` — type of issue (assertion_failure, etc.)
    85→- `repetitions` — if present, shows retry behavior (pass/fail sequence)
    86→- `test_run_id` — the broader test run this execution belongs to
    87→- `crash_report` — crash report data (present when the test runner crashed); contains `exception_type`, `signal`, `exception_subtype`, and `triggered_thread_frames`
    88→
    89→## Code Analysis
    90→
    91→1. Open the file at `failures[0].path` and go to `failures[0].line_number`.
    92→2. Read the full test function and its setup/teardown.
    93→3. Identify which of the common flaky patterns below applies.
    94→4. Check if the test shares state with other tests in the same suite.
    95→
    96→## Common Flaky Patterns
    97→
    98→### Timing and async issues
    99→- **Missing waits**: Test checks a result before an async operation completes. Fix: use `await`, expectations with timeouts, or polling.
   100→- **Race conditions**: Multiple concurrent operations access shared state. Fix: synchronize access or use serial queues.
   101→- **Hardcoded timeouts**: `sleep(1)` or fixed delays that are too short on CI. Fix: use condition-based waits instead of fixed delays.
   102→
   103→### Shared state
   104→- **Test pollution**: One test modifies global/static state that another test depends on. Fix: reset state in setUp/tearDown or use unique instances per test.
   105→- **Singleton contamination**: Shared singletons carry state between tests. Fix: inject dependencies or reset singletons.
   106→- **File system leftovers**: Tests leave files that affect subsequent runs. Fix: use temporary directories and clean up.
   107→
   108→### Environment dependencies
   109→- **Network calls**: Tests hit real services that may be slow or unavailable. Fix: mock network calls.
   110→- **Date/time sensitivity**: Tests depend on current time or timezone. Fix: inject a clock or freeze time.
   111→- **File system paths**: Hardcoded paths that differ between environments. Fix: use relative paths or temp directories.
   112→
   113→### Order dependence
   114→- **Implicit ordering**: Test passes only when run after another test that sets up required state. Fix: make each test self-contained.
   115→- **Parallel execution conflicts**: Tests that work in isolation but fail when run concurrently. Fix: use unique resources per test.
   116→
   117→### Crashes (identified via `crash_report`)
   118→- `EXC_BREAKPOINT` / `SIGTRAP` — force-unwrap of nil, Swift precondition failure
   119→- `EXC_BAD_ACCESS` / `SIGSEGV` — use-after-free or dangling pointer
   120→- `EXC_CRASH` / `SIGABRT` — uncaught Objective-C exception
   121→
   122→## Fix Implementation
   123→
   124→After identifying the pattern:
   125→
   126→1. Apply the smallest fix that addresses the root cause.
   127→2. Do not refactor unrelated code.
   128→3. If the fix requires a test utility (like a mock or helper), check if one already exists before creating a new one.
   129→
   130→## Verification
   131→
   132→### Running tests repeatedly
   133→
   134→Run the specific test repeatedly until failure using `xcodebuild`'s built-in repetition support:
   135→
   136→```bash
   137→xcodebuild test -workspace <workspace> -scheme <scheme> -only-testing <module>/<suite>/<test> -test-iterations <count> -run-tests-until-failure
   138→```
   139→
   140→This runs the test up to `<count>` times and stops at the first failure. Choose the iteration count based on how long the test takes — for fast unit tests use 50–100, for slower integration or acceptance tests use 2–5.
   141→
   142→### Reproducing before fixing
   143→
   144→Before applying a fix, try to reproduce the flaky failure locally. A successful reproduction confirms your root cause analysis and lets you verify the fix directly. Use the "Running tests repeatedly" approach above, or the race condition strategies below if concurrency is suspected.
   145→
   146→Some flaky scenarios — especially race conditions, CI-specific timing issues, or environment-dependent failures — may be difficult or impossible to reproduce locally. If you cannot reproduce after reasonable effort, proceed with fixing based on code analysis and failure logs. A fix backed by clear evidence of a bug (e.g. unsynchronized shared state, TOCTOU pattern) is valid even without local reproduction.
   147→
   148→### Reproducing race conditions
   149→
   150→Race conditions and concurrency bugs often only manifest under CI-level parallelism and are hard to reproduce locally. Try these strategies in order:
   151→
   152→1. **Increase parallelism**: Add `-parallel-testing-enabled YES` to run test suites concurrently.
   153→2. **Run broader test suites**: Instead of running a single test, run the entire module (e.g. `-only-testing ModuleTests`) to increase contention on shared resources.
   154→3. **Thread Sanitizer**: Run with TSan enabled to detect data races deterministically. Note: TSan adds overhead which can change timing, so some races may not trigger under TSan.
   155→
   156→```bash
   157→xcodebuild test -workspace <workspace> -scheme <scheme> -only-testing <module> -enableThreadSanitizer YES
   158→```
   159→
   160→4. **High iteration count with broad scope**: Combine all the above — run the full module with parallelism and many iterations.
   161→
   162→If a race condition cannot be reproduced locally but the code is provably thread-unsafe (e.g. unsynchronized mutation of shared state), the fix is still valid. Verify the fix by confirming the tests pass with the same reproduction strategies above. Document in the commit message that the fix addresses a CI-only race condition identified through code analysis and failure logs.
   163→
   164→## Done Checklist
   165→
   166→- Identified the root cause of flakiness
   167→- Applied a targeted fix
   168→- Verified the test passes consistently (multiple runs)
   169→- Did not introduce new test dependencies or shared state
   170→- Committed the fix with a descriptive message
   171→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→
     4→  alias Anubis.MCP.Error
     5→  alias Tuist.MCP.Authorization
     6→  alias Tuist.Projects
     7→
     8→  def authenticated_subject(assigns) when is_map(assigns) do
     9→    Authorization.authenticated_subject(assigns)
    10→  end
    11→
    12→  def authorize_project(frame, project, action, category, message \\ "You do not have access to this resource.") do
    13→    if Authorization.authorize(authenticated_subject(frame.assigns), action, project, category) do
    14→      :ok
    15→    else
    16→      invalid_params(message, frame)
    17→    end
    18→  end
    19→
    20→  def load_resource({:ok, resource}, _message, _frame), do: {:ok, resource}
    21→  def load_resource({:error, :not_found}, message, frame), do: invalid_params(message, frame)
    22→  def load_resource(_result, message, frame), do: invalid_params(message, frame)
    23→
    24→  def load_project(project_id, frame, message \\ "Project not found.") do
    25→    case Projects.get_project_by_id(project_id) do
    26→      nil -> invalid_params(message, frame)
    27→      project -> {:ok, project}
    28→    end
    29→  end
    30→
    31→  def authorize_project_by_id(
    32→        frame,
    33→        project_id,
    34→        action,
    35→        category,
    36→        unauthorized_message \\ "You do not have access to this resource.",
    37→        not_found_message \\ "Project not found."
    38→      ) do
    39→    with {:ok, project} <- load_project(project_id, frame, not_found_message),
    40→         :ok <- authorize_project(frame, project, action, category, unauthorized_message) do
    41→      {:ok, project}
    42→    end
    43→  end
    44→
    45→  def load_project_by_handle(account_handle, project_handle, frame) do
    46→    case Projects.get_project_by_account_and_project_handles(account_handle, project_handle) do
    47→      nil -> invalid_params("Project not found: #{account_handle}/#{project_handle}", frame)
    48→      project -> {:ok, project}
    49→    end
    50→  end
    51→
    52→  def load_and_authorize_project_by_handle(account_handle, project_handle, frame, action, category, unauthorized_message) do
    53→    with {:ok, project} <- load_project_by_handle(account_handle, project_handle, frame),
    54→         :ok <- authorize_project(frame, project, action, category, unauthorized_message) do
    55→      {:ok, project}
    56→    end
    57→  end
    58→
    59→  def resolve_and_authorize_project(
    60→        %{account_handle: account_handle, project_handle: project_handle},
    61→        frame,
    62→        action,
    63→        category
    64→      )
    65→      when is_binary(account_handle) and is_binary(project_handle) do
    66→    load_and_authorize_project_by_handle(
    67→      account_handle,
    68→      project_handle,
    69→      frame,
    70→      action,
    71→      category,
    72→      "You do not have access to project: #{account_handle}/#{project_handle}"
    73→    )
    74→  end
    75→
    76→  def resolve_and_authorize_project(_arguments, frame, _action, _category) do
    77→    invalid_params("Provide account_handle and project_handle.", frame)
    78→  end
    79→
    80→  def invalid_params(message, frame) do
    81→    {:error, Error.protocol(:invalid_params, %{message: message}), frame}
    82→  end
    83→
    84→  @max_page_size 100
    85→  @default_page_size 20
    86→
    87→  def page(arguments) do
    88→    case Map.get(arguments, :page) do
    89→      value when is_integer(value) and value > 0 -> value
    90→      _ -> 1
    91→    end
    92→  end
    93→
    94→  def page_size(arguments) do
    95→    case Map.get(arguments, :page_size) do
    96→      value when is_integer(value) and value > 0 -> min(value, @max_page_size)
    97→      _ -> @default_page_size
    98→    end
    99→  end
   100→
   101→  def pagination_metadata(meta) do
   102→    %{
   103→      has_next_page: meta.has_next_page?,
   104→      has_previous_page: meta.has_previous_page?,
   105→      total_count: meta.total_count,
   106→      total_pages: meta.total_pages,
   107→      current_page: meta.current_page,
   108→      page_size: meta.page_size
   109→    }
   110→  end
   111→end

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport do
     2→  @moduledoc false
     3→
     4→  alias Tuist.Projects
     5→
     6→  def resolve_project_handles(arguments) do
     7→    case {Map.get(arguments, :account_handle), Map.get(arguments, :project_handle)} do
     8→      {ah, ph} when is_binary(ah) and is_binary(ph) -> {ah, ph}
     9→      _ -> {nil, nil}
    10→    end
    11→  end
    12→
    13→  def resolve_project_metadata(account_handle, project_handle)
    14→      when is_binary(account_handle) and is_binary(project_handle) do
    15→    case Projects.get_project_by_account_and_project_handles(account_handle, project_handle) do
    16→      nil -> %{default_branch: nil, build_system: nil}
    17→      project -> %{default_branch: project.default_branch, build_system: project.build_system}
    18→    end
    19→  end
    20→
    21→  def resolve_project_metadata(_account_handle, _project_handle), do: %{default_branch: nil, build_system: nil}
    22→
    23→  def resolve_default_branch(account_handle, project_handle) do
    24→    resolve_project_metadata(account_handle, project_handle).default_branch
    25→  end
    26→end
    27→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetBundleArtifactTree do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Get the full artifact tree for a bundle as a flat list sorted by path. The bundle_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/bundles/{id}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.Bundles
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    11→
    12→  @authorization_action :read
    13→  @authorization_category :bundle
    14→
    15→  schema do
    16→    field :bundle_id, :string,
    17→      required: true,
    18→      description: "The ID of the bundle."
    19→  end
    20→
    21→  @impl true
    22→  def execute(%{bundle_id: bundle_id}, frame) do
    23→    with {:ok, bundle} <-
    24→           ToolSupport.load_resource(
    25→             Bundles.get_bundle(bundle_id),
    26→             "Bundle not found: #{bundle_id}",
    27→             frame
    28→           ),
    29→         {:ok, _project} <-
    30→           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
    31→             frame,
    32→             bundle.project_id,
    33→             @authorization_action,
    34→             @authorization_category
    35→           ) do
    36→      artifacts = Bundles.get_bundle_artifact_tree(bundle_id)
    37→
    38→      data = %{
    39→        bundle_id: bundle_id,
    40→        artifacts:
    41→          Enum.map(artifacts, fn artifact ->
    42→            %{
    43→              artifact_type: to_string(artifact.artifact_type),
    44→              path: artifact.path,
    45→              size: artifact.size
    46→            }
    47→          end)
    48→      }
    49→
    50→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    51→    end
    52→  end
    53→end
    54→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetCacheRun do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Get detailed information about a specific cache run. The cache_run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/runs/{id}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.CommandEvents
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    11→  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :run
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :cache_run_id, :string,
    18→      required: true,
    19→      description: "The ID of the cache run."
    20→  end
    21→
    22→  @impl true
    23→  def execute(%{cache_run_id: cache_run_id}, frame) do
    24→    with {:ok, event} <-
    25→           ToolSupport.load_resource(
    26→             get_cache_run(cache_run_id),
    27→             "Cache run not found: #{cache_run_id}",
    28→             frame
    29→           ),
    30→         {:ok, _project} <-
    31→           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
    32→             frame,
    33→             event.project_id,
    34→             @authorization_action,
    35→             @authorization_category
    36→           ) do
    37→      data = %{
    38→        id: event.id,
    39→        duration: event.duration,
    40→        status: status_to_string(event.status),
    41→        tuist_version: event.tuist_version,
    42→        swift_version: event.swift_version,
    43→        macos_version: event.macos_version,
    44→        is_ci: event.is_ci,
    45→        git_branch: event.git_branch,
    46→        git_commit_sha: event.git_commit_sha,
    47→        git_ref: event.git_ref,
    48→        command_arguments: event.command_arguments,
    49→        cacheable_targets: event.cacheable_targets,
    50→        local_cache_target_hits: event.local_cache_target_hits,
    51→        remote_cache_target_hits: event.remote_cache_target_hits,
    52→        ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(event.created_at, naive: :utc)
    53→      }
    54→
    55→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    56→    end
    57→  end
    58→
    59→  defp get_cache_run(id) do
    60→    case CommandEvents.get_command_event_by_id(id) do
    61→      {:ok, event} when event.name == "cache" -> {:ok, event}
    62→      {:ok, _event} -> {:error, :not_found}
    63→      error -> error
    64→    end
    65→  end
    66→
    67→  defp status_to_string(0), do: "success"
    68→  defp status_to_string(1), do: "failure"
    69→  defp status_to_string(nil), do: "success"
    70→  defp status_to_string(status) when is_atom(status), do: Atom.to_string(status)
    71→  defp status_to_string(status), do: to_string(status)
    72→end
    73→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCase do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Get detailed information about a test case including reliability and flakiness metrics. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    10→  alias Tuist.Tests
    11→  alias Tuist.Tests.Analytics
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :test
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :test_case_id, :string, description: "The ID of the test case. Required when not using identifier lookup."
    18→
    19→    field :account_handle, :string,
    20→      description: "The account handle (organization or user). Required for identifier lookup."
    21→
    22→    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle. Required for identifier lookup."
    23→
    24→    field :identifier, :string,
    25→      description:
    26→        "Test case identifier in Module/Suite/TestCase or Module/TestCase format. " <>
    27→          "Required when not using test_case_id. Must be combined with account_handle and project_handle."
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  @impl true
    31→  def execute(%{test_case_id: test_case_id}, frame) when is_binary(test_case_id) do
    32→    with {:ok, test_case} <-
    33→           ToolSupport.load_resource(
    34→             Tests.get_test_case_by_id(test_case_id),
    35→             "Test case not found: #{test_case_id}",
    36→             frame
    37→           ),
    38→         {:ok, _project} <-
    39→           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
    40→             frame,
    41→             test_case.project_id,
    42→             @authorization_action,
    43→             @authorization_category
    44→           ) do
    45→      reply_with_test_case(test_case, frame)
    46→    end
    47→  end
    48→
    49→  def execute(%{identifier: identifier} = arguments, frame) when is_binary(identifier) do
    50→    with {:ok, {module_name, suite_name, name}} <- parse_identifier(identifier, frame),
    51→         {:ok, project} <-
    52→           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
    53→             arguments,
    54→             frame,
    55→             @authorization_action,
    56→             @authorization_category
    57→           ),
    58→         {:ok, test_case} <- find_test_case_by_name(project.id, module_name, suite_name, name, frame) do
    59→      reply_with_test_case(test_case, frame)
    60→    end
    61→  end
    62→
    63→  def execute(_arguments, frame) do
    64→    ToolSupport.invalid_params(
    65→      "Provide either test_case_id, or identifier with account_handle and project_handle.",
    66→      frame
    67→    )
    68→  end
    69→
    70→  defp parse_identifier(identifier, frame) do
    71→    case String.split(identifier, "/") do
    72→      [module_name, suite_name, name] -> {:ok, {module_name, suite_name, name}}
    73→      [module_name, name] -> {:ok, {module_name, nil, name}}
    74→      _ -> ToolSupport.invalid_params("Invalid identifier format. Use Module/Suite/TestCase or Module/TestCase.", frame)
    75→    end
    76→  end
    77→
    78→  defp find_test_case_by_name(project_id, module_name, suite_name, name, frame) do
    79→    filters =
    80→      [
    81→        %{field: :module_name, op: :==, value: module_name},
    82→        %{field: :name, op: :==, value: name}
    83→      ] ++ if(suite_name, do: [%{field: :suite_name, op: :==, value: suite_name}], else: [])
    84→
    85→    {test_cases, _meta} =
    86→      Tests.list_test_cases(project_id, %{
    87→        filters: filters,
    88→        page: 1,
    89→        page_size: 1
    90→      })
    91→
    92→    case test_cases do
    93→      [test_case | _] -> {:ok, test_case}
    94→      [] -> ToolSupport.invalid_params("Test case not found: #{module_name}/#{suite_name || name}", frame)
    95→    end
    96→  end
    97→
    98→  defp reply_with_test_case(test_case, frame) do
    99→    analytics = Analytics.test_case_analytics_by_id(test_case.id)
   100→    reliability_rate = Analytics.test_case_reliability_by_id(test_case.id, "main")
   101→    flakiness_rate = Analytics.get_test_case_flakiness_rate(test_case)
   102→
   103→    data = %{
   104→      id: test_case.id,
   105→      name: test_case.name,
   106→      module_name: test_case.module_name,
   107→      suite_name: test_case.suite_name,
   108→      is_flaky: test_case.is_flaky,
   109→      is_quarantined: test_case.is_quarantined,
   110→      last_status: to_string(test_case.last_status),
   111→      last_duration: test_case.last_duration,
   112→      avg_duration: test_case.avg_duration,
   113→      reliability_rate: reliability_rate,
   114→      flakiness_rate: flakiness_rate,
   115→      total_runs: analytics.total_count,
   116→      failed_runs: analytics.failed_count
   117→    }
   118→
   119→    {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
   120→  end
   121→end
   122→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCaseRunAttachments do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  List attachments for a test case run. Each attachment includes a temporary download URL (valid for 1 hour).
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    10→  alias Tuist.Storage
    11→  alias Tuist.Tests
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :test
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :test_case_run_id, :string,
    18→      required: true,
    19→      description: "The ID of the test case run."
    20→  end
    21→
    22→  @impl true
    23→  def execute(%{test_case_run_id: test_case_run_id}, frame) do
    24→    with {:ok, run} <-
    25→           ToolSupport.load_resource(
    26→             Tests.get_test_case_run_by_id(test_case_run_id, preload: [:attachments]),
    27→             "Test case run not found: #{test_case_run_id}",
    28→             frame
    29→           ),
    30→         {:ok, project} <-
    31→           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
    32→             frame,
    33→             run.project_id,
    34→             @authorization_action,
    35→             @authorization_category
    36→           ) do
    37→      data = %{
    38→        test_case_run_id: test_case_run_id,
    39→        attachments:
    40→          Enum.map(run.attachments || [], fn attachment ->
    41→            s3_object_key =
    42→              Tests.attachment_storage_key(%{
    43→                account_handle: project.account.name,
    44→                project_handle: project.name,
    45→                test_case_run_id: test_case_run_id,
    46→                attachment_id: attachment.id,
    47→                file_name: attachment.file_name
    48→              })
    49→
    50→            %{
    51→              id: attachment.id,
    52→              file_name: attachment.file_name,
    53→              type: attachment_type(attachment.file_name),
    54→              download_url: Storage.generate_download_url(s3_object_key, project.account, expires_in: 3600)
    55→            }
    56→          end)
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    60→    end
    61→  end
    62→
    63→  defp attachment_type(file_name) do
    64→    ext = file_name |> String.downcase() |> Path.extname()
    65→
    66→    cond do
    67→      ext in [".png", ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".gif", ".webp", ".heic"] -> "image"
    68→      ext in [".txt"] -> "text"
    69→      ext in [".log"] -> "log"
    70→      ext in [".json"] -> "json"
    71→      ext in [".xml"] -> "xml"
    72→      ext in [".csv"] -> "csv"
    73→      ext in [".ips"] -> "crash_report"
    74→      true -> "file"
    75→    end
    76→  end
    77→end
    78→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCaseRuns do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  List test case runs, optionally filtered by test case or test run. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
    11→  alias Tuist.Tests
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :test
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :account_handle, :string,
    18→      required: true,
    19→      description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    20→
    21→    field :project_handle, :string,
    22→      required: true,
    23→      description: "The project handle."
    24→
    25→    field :test_case_id, :string, description: "Filter by test case ID."
    26→    field :test_run_id, :string, description: "Filter by test run ID."
    27→    field :flaky, :boolean, description: "When true, returns only flaky runs."
    28→    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
    29→    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
    30→  end
    31→
    32→  @impl true
    33→  def execute(arguments, frame) do
    34→    with {:ok, project} <-
    35→           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
    36→             arguments,
    37→             frame,
    38→             @authorization_action,
    39→             @authorization_category
    40→           ) do
    41→      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
    42→      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
    43→      filters = build_filters(project.id, arguments)
    44→
    45→      {runs, meta} =
    46→        Tests.list_test_case_runs(%{
    47→          filters: filters,
    48→          order_by: [:inserted_at],
    49→          order_directions: [:desc],
    50→          page: page,
    51→          page_size: page_size
    52→        })
    53→
    54→      data = %{
    55→        test_case_runs:
    56→          Enum.map(runs, fn run ->
    57→            %{
    58→              id: run.id,
    59→              test_case_id: run.test_case_id,
    60→              test_run_id: run.test_run_id,
    61→              name: run.name,
    62→              module_name: run.module_name,
    63→              suite_name: run.suite_name,
    64→              status: to_string(run.status),
    65→              duration: run.duration,
    66→              is_ci: run.is_ci,
    67→              is_flaky: run.is_flaky,
    68→              git_branch: run.git_branch,
    69→              git_commit_sha: run.git_commit_sha,
    70→              ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(run.ran_at, naive: :utc)
    71→            }
    72→          end),
    73→        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
    74→      }
    75→
    76→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    77→    end
    78→  end
    79→
    80→  defp build_filters(project_id, arguments) do
    81→    base = [%{field: :project_id, op: :==, value: project_id}]
    82→
    83→    base =
    84→      case Map.get(arguments, :test_case_id) do
    85→        nil -> base
    86→        value -> base ++ [%{field: :test_case_id, op: :==, value: value}]
    87→      end
    88→
    89→    base =
    90→      case Map.get(arguments, :test_run_id) do
    91→        nil -> base
    92→        value -> base ++ [%{field: :test_run_id, op: :==, value: value}]
    93→      end
    94→
    95→    if Map.get(arguments, :flaky) do
    96→      base ++ [%{field: :is_flaky, op: :==, value: true}]
    97→    else
    98→      base
    99→    end
   100→  end
   101→end
   102→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCases do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  List test cases for a project. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Formatter
    11→  alias Tuist.Tests
    12→
    13→  @authorization_action :read
    14→  @authorization_category :test
    15→
    16→  schema do
    17→    field :account_handle, :string,
    18→      required: true,
    19→      description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    20→
    21→    field :project_handle, :string,
    22→      required: true,
    23→      description: "The project handle."
    24→
    25→    field :flaky, :boolean, description: "When true, returns only flaky test cases."
    26→    field :quarantined, :boolean, description: "Filter by quarantined status."
    27→    field :module_name, :string, description: "Filter by module name."
    28→    field :name, :string, description: "Filter by test case name."
    29→    field :suite_name, :string, description: "Filter by suite name."
    30→    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
    31→    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
    32→  end
    33→
    34→  @impl true
    35→  def execute(arguments, frame) do
    36→    with {:ok, project} <-
    37→           ToolSupport.resolve_and_authorize_project(
    38→             arguments,
    39→             frame,
    40→             @authorization_action,
    41→             @authorization_category
    42→           ) do
    43→      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
    44→      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
    45→      filters = build_filters(arguments)
    46→
    47→      {test_cases, meta} =
    48→        Tests.list_test_cases(project.id, %{
    49→          filters: filters,
    50→          order_by: [:last_ran_at],
    51→          order_directions: [:desc],
    52→          page: page,
    53→          page_size: page_size
    54→        })
    55→
    56→      data = %{
    57→        test_cases:
    58→          Enum.map(test_cases, fn test_case ->
    59→            %{
    60→              id: test_case.id,
    61→              name: test_case.name,
    62→              module_name: test_case.module_name,
    63→              suite_name: test_case.suite_name,
    64→              is_flaky: test_case.is_flaky,
    65→              is_quarantined: test_case.is_quarantined,
    66→              last_status: to_string(test_case.last_status),
    67→              last_duration: test_case.last_duration,
    68→              last_ran_at: Formatter.iso8601(test_case.last_ran_at, naive: :utc),
    69→              avg_duration: test_case.avg_duration
    70→            }
    71→          end),
    72→        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
    73→      }
    74→
    75→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    76→    end
    77→  end
    78→
    79→  defp build_filters(arguments) do
    80→    arguments
    81→    |> Map.take([:flaky, :quarantined, :module_name, :name, :suite_name])
    82→    |> Enum.reduce([], fn
    83→      {:flaky, value}, filters -> maybe_add_filter(filters, :is_flaky, value)
    84→      {:quarantined, value}, filters -> maybe_add_filter(filters, :is_quarantined, value)
    85→      {field, value}, filters -> maybe_add_filter(filters, field, value)
    86→    end)
    87→    |> Enum.reverse()
    88→  end
    89→
    90→  defp maybe_add_filter(filters, _field, nil), do: filters
    91→
    92→  defp maybe_add_filter(filters, field, true) when field in [:is_flaky, :is_quarantined],
    93→    do: [%{field: field, op: :==, value: true} | filters]
    94→
    95→  defp maybe_add_filter(filters, field, value), do: [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value} | filters]
    96→end
    97→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestSuiteRuns do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  List test suite runs for a specific test run, optionally filtered by module. The test_run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/tests/test-runs/{id}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    10→  alias Tuist.Tests
    11→
    12→  @authorization_action :read
    13→  @authorization_category :test
    14→
    15→  schema do
    16→    field :test_run_id, :string,
    17→      required: true,
    18→      description: "The ID of the test run."
    19→
    20→    field :module_name, :string, description: "Filter suites by module name."
    21→    field :status, :string, description: "Filter by status: success, failure, or skipped."
    22→    field :page, :integer, description: "Page number (default: 1)."
    23→    field :page_size, :integer, description: "Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)."
    24→  end
    25→
    26→  @impl true
    27→  def execute(%{test_run_id: test_run_id} = arguments, frame) do
    28→    with {:ok, run} <-
    29→           ToolSupport.load_resource(
    30→             Tests.get_test(test_run_id),
    31→             "Test run not found: #{test_run_id}",
    32→             frame
    33→           ),
    34→         {:ok, _project} <-
    35→           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
    36→             frame,
    37→             run.project_id,
    38→             @authorization_action,
    39→             @authorization_category
    40→           ) do
    41→      filters = [%{field: :test_run_id, op: :==, value: test_run_id}]
    42→
    43→      filters =
    44→        Enum.reduce([:status], filters, fn field, acc ->
    45→          case Map.get(arguments, field) do
    46→            nil -> acc
    47→            value -> acc ++ [%{field: field, op: :==, value: value}]
    48→          end
    49→        end)
    50→
    51→      page = ToolSupport.page(arguments)
    52→      page_size = ToolSupport.page_size(arguments)
    53→
    54→      {suites, meta} =
    55→        Tests.list_test_suite_runs(%{
    56→          filters: filters,
    57→          order_by: [:duration],
    58→          order_directions: [:desc],
    59→          page: page,
    60→          page_size: page_size
    61→        })
    62→
    63→      suites =
    64→        case Map.get(arguments, :module_name) do
    65→          nil ->
    66→            suites
    67→
    68→          module_name ->
    69→            module_ids = get_module_run_ids(test_run_id, module_name)
    70→            Enum.filter(suites, &(&1.test_module_run_id in module_ids))
    71→        end
    72→
    73→      data = %{
    74→        suites:
    75→          Enum.map(suites, fn suite ->
    76→            %{
    77→              name: suite.name,
    78→              status: to_string(suite.status),
    79→              is_flaky: suite.is_flaky,
    80→              duration: suite.duration,
    81→              test_case_count: suite.test_case_count,
    82→              avg_test_case_duration: suite.avg_test_case_duration
    83→            }
    84→          end),
    85→        pagination_metadata: ToolSupport.pagination_metadata(meta)
    86→      }
    87→
    88→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    89→    end
    90→  end
    91→
    92→  defp get_module_run_ids(test_run_id, module_name) do
    93→    {modules, _meta} =
    94→      Tests.list_test_module_runs(%{
    95→        filters: [
    96→          %{field: :test_run_id, op: :==, value: test_run_id},
    97→          %{field: :name, op: :==, value: module_name}
    98→        ],
    99→        page: 1,
   100→        page_size: 10
   101→      })
   102→
   103→    Enum.map(modules, & &1.id)
   104→  end
   105→end
   106→

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuildIssues do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  List build issues (warnings and errors) for a specific build run. The build_run_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/builds/build-runs/{id}.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.Builds
    10→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport
    11→
    12→  @authorization_action :read
    13→  @authorization_category :build
    14→
    15→  schema do
    16→    field :build_run_id, :string,
    17→      required: true,
    18→      description: "The ID of the build run."
    19→
    20→    field :type, :string, description: "Filter by issue type: warning or error."
    21→    field :target, :string, description: "Filter by target name."
    22→    field :step_type, :string, description: "Filter by compilation step type (e.g. swift_compilation, linker)."
    23→  end
    24→
    25→  @impl true
    26→  def execute(%{build_run_id: build_run_id} = arguments, frame) do
    27→    with {:ok, build} <-
    28→           ToolSupport.load_resource(
    29→             get_build(build_run_id),
    30→             "Build not found: #{build_run_id}",
    31→             frame
    32→           ),
    33→         {:ok, _project} <-
    34→           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
    35→             frame,
    36→             build.project_id,
    37→             @authorization_action,
    38→             @authorization_category
    39→           ) do
    40→      issues = Builds.list_build_issues(build_run_id)
    41→
    42→      issues =
    43→        issues
    44→        |> maybe_filter(:type, Map.get(arguments, :type))
    45→        |> maybe_filter(:target, Map.get(arguments, :target))
    46→        |> maybe_filter(:step_type, Map.get(arguments, :step_type))
    47→
    48→      data =
    49→        Enum.map(issues, fn issue ->
    50→          %{
    51→            type: to_string(issue.type),
    52→            target: issue.target,
    53→            project: issue.project,
    54→            title: issue.title,
    55→            message: issue.message,
    56→            signature: issue.signature,
    57→            step_type: to_string(issue.step_type),
    58→            path: issue.path,
    59→            starting_line: issue.starting_line,
    60→            ending_line: issue.ending_line,
    61→            starting_column: issue.starting_column,
    62→            ending_column: issue.ending_column
    63→          }
    64→        end)
    65→
    66→      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    67→    end
    68→  end
    69→
    70→  defp get_build(id) do
    71→    case Builds.get_build(id) do
    72→      nil -> {:error, :not_found}
    73→      build -> {:ok, build}
    74→    end
    75→  end
    76→
    77→  defp maybe_filter(items, _field, nil), do: items
    78→
    79→  defp maybe_filter(items, field, value) do
    80→    Enum.filter(items, fn item -> to_string(Map.get(item, field)) == value end)
    81→  end
    82→end
    83→
